question_html: Find the area of the parallelogram whose adjacent sides are determined by the vectors {tex}\vec a = \hat i - \hat j + 3\hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec b = 2\hat i - 7\hat j + \hat k{/tex}
question_text: Find the area of the parallelogram whose adjacent sides are determined by the vectors {tex}\vec a = \hat i - \hat j + 3\hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec b = 2\hat i - 7\hat j + \hat k{/tex}
explanation: Given: Vectors representing two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are {tex}\vec a = \hat i - \hat j + 3\hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec b = 2\hat i - 7\hat j + \hat k{/tex} {tex}\therefore \vec a \times \vec b = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\hat i}&\hat j&{\hat k} \\ 1&{ - 1}&3 \\ 2&{ - 7}&1 \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = {tex}\hat i\left( { - 1 + 21} \right) - \hat j\left( {1 - 6} \right) + \hat k\left( { - 7 + 2} \right){/tex} = {tex}20\hat i + 5\hat j - 5\hat k{/tex} Now Area of parallelogram = {tex}\left| {\vec a \times \vec b} \right|{/tex} = {tex} \sqrt {400 + 25 + 25} = \sqrt {450} = 15\sqrt 2 {/tex} sq. units
solution: Given: Vectors representing two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are {tex}\vec a = \hat i - \hat j + 3\hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec b = 2\hat i - 7\hat j + \hat k{/tex} {tex}\therefore \vec a \times \vec b = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\hat i}&\hat j&{\hat k} \\ 1&{ - 1}&3 \\ 2&{ - 7}&1 \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = {tex}\hat i\left( { - 1 + 21} \right) - \hat j\left( {1 - 6} \right) + \hat k\left( { - 7 + 2} \right){/tex} = {tex}20\hat i + 5\hat j - 5\hat k{/tex} Now Area of parallelogram = {tex}\left| {\vec a \times \vec b} \right|{/tex} = {tex} \sqrt {400 + 25 + 25} = \sqrt {450} = 15\sqrt 2 {/tex} sq. units
solution_html:
Given: Vectors representing two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are
{tex}\vec a = \hat i - \hat j + 3\hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec b = 2\hat i - 7\hat j + \hat k{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \vec a \times \vec b = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\hat i}&\hat j&{\hat k} \\ 1&{ - 1}&3 \\ 2&{ - 7}&1 \end{array}} \right| {/tex} = {tex}\hat i\left( { - 1 + 21} \right) - \hat j\left( {1 - 6} \right) + \hat k\left( { - 7 + 2} \right){/tex} = {tex}20\hat i + 5\hat j - 5\hat k{/tex}
Now Area of parallelogram = {tex}\left| {\vec a \times \vec b} \right|{/tex}
= {tex} \sqrt {400 + 25 + 25} = \sqrt {450} = 15\sqrt 2 {/tex} sq. units

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Three vectors {tex}\vec{a}, \vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec c{/tex} satisfy the condition {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex}. Evaluate the quantity {tex}\mu=\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}{/tex}, if {tex}|\vec{a}|=3,|\vec{b}|=4{/tex} and {tex}|\vec{c}|=2{/tex}.
question_text: Three vectors {tex}\vec{a}, \vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec c{/tex} satisfy the condition {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex} . Evaluate the quantity {tex}\mu=\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}{/tex} , if {tex}|\vec{a}|=3,|\vec{b}|=4{/tex} and {tex}|\vec{c}|=2{/tex} .
explanation: Since {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex} , we have {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0}=0{/tex} or {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{a}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}=0{/tex} Therefore {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}=-|\vec{a}|^{2}=-9{/tex} .....(i) Again {tex}\vec{b} \cdot(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c})=0{/tex} or {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}=-|\vec{b}|^{2}=-16{/tex} .....(ii) Similarly {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}=-4{/tex} ......(iii) Adding (i), (ii) and (iii), we have {tex}2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c})=-29{/tex} or {tex}2 \mu=-29{/tex} i.e. {tex}\mu=\frac{-29}{2}{/tex}
solution: Since {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex} , we have {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0}=0{/tex} or {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{a}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}=0{/tex} Therefore {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}=-|\vec{a}|^{2}=-9{/tex} .....(i) Again {tex}\vec{b} \cdot(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c})=0{/tex} or {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}=-|\vec{b}|^{2}=-16{/tex} .....(ii) Similarly {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}=-4{/tex} ......(iii) Adding (i), (ii) and (iii), we have {tex}2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c})=-29{/tex} or {tex}2 \mu=-29{/tex} i.e. {tex}\mu=\frac{-29}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Since {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex}, we have
{tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0}=0{/tex} 
or {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{a}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}=0{/tex} 
Therefore {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}=-|\vec{a}|^{2}=-9{/tex} .....(i)
Again {tex}\vec{b} \cdot(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c})=0{/tex} 
or {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}=-|\vec{b}|^{2}=-16{/tex} .....(ii)
Similarly {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}=-4{/tex} ......(iii)
Adding (i), (ii) and (iii), we have
{tex}2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c})=-29{/tex} 
or {tex}2 \mu=-29{/tex} i.e. {tex}\mu=\frac{-29}{2}{/tex}

question_html: For two non-zero vectors {tex}\vec {\text a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {\text b}{/tex}, if {tex}|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|{/tex}, then find the angle between {tex}\vec {\text a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {\text b}{/tex}.
question_text: For two non-zero vectors {tex}\vec {\text a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {\text b}{/tex} , if {tex}|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|{/tex} , then find the angle between {tex}\vec {\text a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {\text b}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}|\vec {\text a} - \vec {\text b}|^2{/tex} = {tex}|\vec {\text a} + \vec {\text b}|^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}|\vec {\text a}|^2 - 2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+|\vec{b}|^2{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}|^2+2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+|\vec{b}|^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} angle between {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex} is 90 o .
solution: {tex}|\vec {\text a} - \vec {\text b}|^2{/tex} = {tex}|\vec {\text a} + \vec {\text b}|^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}|\vec {\text a}|^2 - 2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+|\vec{b}|^2{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}|^2+2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+|\vec{b}|^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} angle between {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex} is 90 o .
solution_html:
{tex}|\vec {\text a} - \vec {\text b}|^2{/tex} = {tex}|\vec {\text a} + \vec {\text b}|^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}|\vec {\text a}|^2 - 2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+|\vec{b}|^2{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}|^2+2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+|\vec{b}|^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} angle between {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex} is 90o.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{\text a}, \vec{\text b}, \vec{\text c}{/tex} are three non-zero unequal vectors such that {tex}\vec{\text a} \cdot \vec{\text b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{\text a} \cdot \vec{\text c}{/tex}, then find the angle between {tex}\vec{\text a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\text b}-\vec{\text c}{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{\text a}, \vec{\text b}, \vec{\text c}{/tex} are three non-zero unequal vectors such that {tex}\vec{\text a} \cdot \vec{\text b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{\text a} \cdot \vec{\text c}{/tex} , then find the angle between {tex}\vec{\text a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\text b}-\vec{\text c}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{0}{/tex} ; {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} or {tex}\vec{a} \perp(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} As, {tex}\vec{a} \neq \vec{0}{/tex} ; {tex}\vec{b} \neq \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} the angle between {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{0}{/tex} ; {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} or {tex}\vec{a} \perp(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} As, {tex}\vec{a} \neq \vec{0}{/tex} ; {tex}\vec{b} \neq \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} the angle between {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{0}{/tex}; {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} or {tex}\vec{a} \perp(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex}
As, {tex}\vec{a} \neq \vec{0}{/tex}; {tex}\vec{b} \neq \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} the angle between {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}

question_html: If points A, B and C have position vectors {tex}2 \hat{\text i}, \hat{\text j}{/tex} and {tex}2 \hat{\text k}{/tex} respectively, then show that {tex}\triangle \mathrm{ABC}{/tex} is an isosceles triangle.
question_text: If points A, B and C have position vectors {tex}2 \hat{\text i}, \hat{\text j}{/tex} and {tex}2 \hat{\text k}{/tex} respectively, then show that {tex}\triangle \mathrm{ABC}{/tex} is an isosceles triangle.
explanation: {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BA}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathbf{i}}-\hat{\mathbf{j}} {/tex} ; {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathbf{k}}-\hat{\mathbf{j}}{/tex} {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BA}}|{/tex} = {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}|{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\Delta \mathrm{ABC}{/tex} is an isosceles triangle
solution: {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BA}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathbf{i}}-\hat{\mathbf{j}} {/tex} ; {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathbf{k}}-\hat{\mathbf{j}}{/tex} {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BA}}|{/tex} = {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}|{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\Delta \mathrm{ABC}{/tex} is an isosceles triangle
solution_html:
{tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BA}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathbf{i}}-\hat{\mathbf{j}} {/tex}; {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathbf{k}}-\hat{\mathbf{j}}{/tex}
{tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BA}}|{/tex} = {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}|{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\Delta \mathrm{ABC}{/tex} is an isosceles triangle

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If the projection of the vector {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} on the vector {tex}p \hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}-2 \hat{\text k}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}, then find the value(s) of p.
question_text: If the projection of the vector {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} on the vector {tex}p \hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}-2 \hat{\text k}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} , then find the value(s) of p.
explanation: Here, {tex}\left[\frac{(\hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}+\hat{\text k}) \cdot(\text p \hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}-2 \hat{\text k})}{\sqrt{\text p^2+1+4}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac 13{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{p-1}{\sqrt{p^2+5}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac 13{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8p 2 – 18p + 4 = 0 4p 2 – 9p + 2 = 0 4p 2 – 8p – p + 2 = 0 (4p – 1) (p – 2) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = 2 or p = {tex}\frac 14{/tex}
solution: Here, {tex}\left[\frac{(\hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}+\hat{\text k}) \cdot(\text p \hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}-2 \hat{\text k})}{\sqrt{\text p^2+1+4}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac 13{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{p-1}{\sqrt{p^2+5}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac 13{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8p 2 – 18p + 4 = 0 4p 2 – 9p + 2 = 0 4p 2 – 8p – p + 2 = 0 (4p – 1) (p – 2) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = 2 or p = {tex}\frac 14{/tex}
solution_html:
Here, {tex}\left[\frac{(\hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}+\hat{\text k}) \cdot(\text p \hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}-2 \hat{\text k})}{\sqrt{\text p^2+1+4}}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac 13{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{p-1}{\sqrt{p^2+5}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac 13{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8p2 – 18p + 4 = 0
4p2 – 9p + 2 = 0
4p2 – 8p – p + 2 = 0
(4p – 1) (p – 2) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = 2 or p = {tex}\frac 14{/tex}

question_html:
Position vectors of the points A, B and C as shown in the figure below are {tex}\vec {\text a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec {\text b}{/tex}, and {tex}\vec {\text c}{/tex} respectively.

If {tex}\overrightarrow {\text {AC}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac 54{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow {\text {AB}}{/tex}, express {tex}\vec {\text c}{/tex} in terms of {tex}\vec {\text a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {\text b}{/tex}.
question_text: Position vectors of the points A, B and C as shown in the figure below are {tex}\vec {\text a}{/tex} , {tex}\vec {\text b}{/tex} , and {tex}\vec {\text c}{/tex} respectively. If {tex}\overrightarrow {\text {AC}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac 54{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow {\text {AB}}{/tex} , express {tex}\vec {\text c}{/tex} in terms of {tex}\vec {\text a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {\text b}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\vec{\text c}-\vec{\text a}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5}{4}(\vec{\text b}-\vec{\text a}){/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\vec{\text c}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5 \vec{\text b}}{4}-\frac{\vec{\text a}}{4}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\vec{\text c}-\vec{\text a}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5}{4}(\vec{\text b}-\vec{\text a}){/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\vec{\text c}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5 \vec{\text b}}{4}-\frac{\vec{\text a}}{4}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\vec{\text c}-\vec{\text a}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5}{4}(\vec{\text b}-\vec{\text a}){/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\vec{\text c}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5 \vec{\text b}}{4}-\frac{\vec{\text a}}{4}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex} \vec { a } = 4 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k },{/tex} then find a unit vector along the vector {tex} \vec { a } \times \vec { b }.{/tex}
question_text: If {tex} \vec { a } = 4 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k },{/tex} then find a unit vector along the vector {tex} \vec { a } \times \vec { b }.{/tex}
explanation: {tex}\begin{equation} \vec{a} \times \vec{b} \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{\imath} & \hat{\jmath} & \widehat{k} \\ \mathbf{4} & -\mathbf{1} & \mathbf{1} \\ \mathbf{2} & -\mathbf{2} & \mathbf{1} \end{array}\right| \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-6 \hat{k} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}|{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{41}{/tex} unit vector along {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{41}}(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-6 \hat{k}){/tex}
solution: {tex}\begin{equation} \vec{a} \times \vec{b} \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{\imath} & \hat{\jmath} & \widehat{k} \\ \mathbf{4} & -\mathbf{1} & \mathbf{1} \\ \mathbf{2} & -\mathbf{2} & \mathbf{1} \end{array}\right| \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-6 \hat{k} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}|{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{41}{/tex} unit vector along {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{41}}(\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}-6 \hat{k}){/tex}
solution_html:

question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} are three vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}|{/tex} = 7, {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex} = 24, {tex}|\vec{c}|{/tex} = 25 and {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{0}{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} are three vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}|{/tex} = 7, {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex} = 24, {tex}|\vec{c}|{/tex} = 25 and {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{0}{/tex} , then find the value of {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{0}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c})^2{/tex} = {tex}(\vec{0})^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}|^2+|\vec{b}|^2+|\vec{c}|^2{/tex} + {tex}2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 49 + 576 + 625 + {tex}2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}{/tex} = -625
solution: {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{0}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c})^2{/tex} = {tex}(\vec{0})^2{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}|^2+|\vec{b}|^2+|\vec{c}|^2{/tex} + {tex}2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 49 + 576 + 625 + {tex}2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}{/tex} = -625
solution_html:
{tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{0}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c})^2{/tex} = {tex}(\vec{0})^2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}|^2+|\vec{b}|^2+|\vec{c}|^2{/tex} + {tex}2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}){/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 49 + 576 + 625 + {tex}2(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}){/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} + {tex}\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}{/tex} = -625

question_html: For the vectors {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}, verify that the angle between {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}.
question_text: For the vectors {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} , verify that the angle between {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 1 & -2 & 3 \\ 2 & 3 & -5 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}+11 \hat{j}+7 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot(\vec{a} \times \vec{b}){/tex} = 1 - 22 + 21 = 0 {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{a} \times \vec{b}| \cos \theta{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cos {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 1 & -2 & 3 \\ 2 & 3 & -5 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}+11 \hat{j}+7 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot(\vec{a} \times \vec{b}){/tex} = 1 - 22 + 21 = 0 {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{a} \times \vec{b}| \cos \theta{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} cos {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 1 & -2 & 3 \\ 2 & 3 & -5 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}+11 \hat{j}+7 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{a} \cdot(\vec{a} \times \vec{b}){/tex} = 1 - 22 + 21 = 0

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find a vector of magnitude 6, which is perpendicular to each of the vectors {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a}-\vec{b}{/tex}, where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex}.
question_text: Find a vector of magnitude 6, which is perpendicular to each of the vectors {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a}-\vec{b}{/tex} , where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} .
explanation: Let {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex} , and {tex}\vec{d}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a}-\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}-\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & -2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}-2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}|\vec{c} \times \vec{d}|=\sqrt{24}{/tex} Required vector = {tex}6\left(\frac{\vec{c} \times \vec{d}}{|\vec{c} \times \vec{d}|}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{6}{\sqrt{24}}(-2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}){/tex} or {tex}\sqrt{6}(-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex}
solution: Let {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex} , and {tex}\vec{d}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a}-\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}-\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & -2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}-2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}|\vec{c} \times \vec{d}|=\sqrt{24}{/tex} Required vector = {tex}6\left(\frac{\vec{c} \times \vec{d}}{|\vec{c} \times \vec{d}|}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{6}{\sqrt{24}}(-2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}){/tex} or {tex}\sqrt{6}(-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex}, and {tex}\vec{d}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a}-\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}-\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & -1 & -2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}-2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}|\vec{c} \times \vec{d}|=\sqrt{24}{/tex}
Required vector = {tex}6\left(\frac{\vec{c} \times \vec{d}}{|\vec{c} \times \vec{d}|}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\frac{6}{\sqrt{24}}(-2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}){/tex} or {tex}\sqrt{6}(-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If three non-zero vectors are {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} such that {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}{/tex}, then show that {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{c}{/tex}.
question_text: If three non-zero vectors are {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} such that {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}{/tex} , then show that {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot(\vec{b}-\vec{c}) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \perp(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} as {tex}\vec{a} \neq 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \perp(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} ...(i) Again, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \times(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \|(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} . as {tex}\vec{a} \neq 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \|(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} ...(ii) from 1 and 2, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} ( {tex}\because{/tex} can't be parallel and perpendicular to {tex}(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} simultaneously.)
solution: {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot(\vec{b}-\vec{c}) = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \perp(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} as {tex}\vec{a} \neq 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \perp(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} ...(i) Again, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \times(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \|(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} . as {tex}\vec{a} \neq 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \|(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} ...(ii) from 1 and 2, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} ( {tex}\because{/tex} can't be parallel and perpendicular to {tex}(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} simultaneously.)
solution_html:
{tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot(\vec{b}-\vec{c}) = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \perp(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex}
as {tex}\vec{a} \neq 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \perp(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} ...(i)
Again, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \times(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \|(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex}.
as {tex}\vec{a} \neq 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{c}{/tex} = 0 or {tex}\vec{a} \|(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} ...(ii)
from 1 and 2, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{c}{/tex}({tex}\because{/tex} can't be parallel and perpendicular to {tex}(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} simultaneously.)

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec {\text r}{/tex} = {tex}3 \hat{\text i}-2 \hat{\text j}+6 \hat{\text k}{/tex}, find the value of {tex}(\vec{\text r} \times \hat{\text j}) \cdot(\vec{\text r} \times \hat{\text k}){/tex} - 12.
question_text: If {tex}\vec {\text r}{/tex} = {tex}3 \hat{\text i}-2 \hat{\text j}+6 \hat{\text k}{/tex} , find the value of {tex}(\vec{\text r} \times \hat{\text j}) \cdot(\vec{\text r} \times \hat{\text k}){/tex} - 12.
explanation: {tex}(\vec{\text r} \times \hat{\text j}) \cdot(\vec{\text r} \times \hat{\text k})-12{/tex} = {tex}(3 \hat{\text k}-6 \hat{\text i}) \cdot(-3 \hat{\text j}-2 \hat{\text i})-12{/tex} = 12 - 12 = 0
solution: {tex}(\vec{\text r} \times \hat{\text j}) \cdot(\vec{\text r} \times \hat{\text k})-12{/tex} = {tex}(3 \hat{\text k}-6 \hat{\text i}) \cdot(-3 \hat{\text j}-2 \hat{\text i})-12{/tex} = 12 - 12 = 0
solution_html:
{tex}(\vec{\text r} \times \hat{\text j}) \cdot(\vec{\text r} \times \hat{\text k})-12{/tex} = {tex}(3 \hat{\text k}-6 \hat{\text i}) \cdot(-3 \hat{\text j}-2 \hat{\text i})-12{/tex}
= 12 - 12 = 0

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\lambda {/tex}
question_text: If {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} , then find the value of {tex}\lambda {/tex}
explanation: Given: {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} Now {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k + \lambda \left( { - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k} \right){/tex} = {tex} = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k - \lambda \hat i + 2\lambda \hat j + \lambda \hat k{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec a + \lambda \vec b = \left( {2 - \lambda } \right)\hat i + \left( {2 + 2\lambda } \right)\hat j + \left( {3 + \lambda } \right)\hat k{/tex} Again, {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j = 3\hat i + \hat j + 0\hat k{/tex} Since, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} therefore, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right).\vec c = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6 - 3\lambda + 2 + 2\lambda = 0 \Rightarrow - \lambda + 8 = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow - \lambda = - 8 \Rightarrow \lambda = 8{/tex}
solution: Given: {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} Now {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k + \lambda \left( { - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k} \right){/tex} = {tex} = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k - \lambda \hat i + 2\lambda \hat j + \lambda \hat k{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec a + \lambda \vec b = \left( {2 - \lambda } \right)\hat i + \left( {2 + 2\lambda } \right)\hat j + \left( {3 + \lambda } \right)\hat k{/tex} Again, {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j = 3\hat i + \hat j + 0\hat k{/tex} Since, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} therefore, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right).\vec c = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6 - 3\lambda + 2 + 2\lambda = 0 \Rightarrow - \lambda + 8 = 0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow - \lambda = - 8 \Rightarrow \lambda = 8{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}\vec a = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k,\vec b = - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k{/tex} and {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j{/tex} 

Now {tex}\vec a + \lambda \vec b = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k + \lambda \left( { - \hat i + 2\hat j + \hat k} \right){/tex}=

{tex} = 2\hat i + 2\hat j + 3\hat k - \lambda \hat i + 2\lambda \hat j + \lambda \hat k{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \vec a + \lambda \vec b = \left( {2 - \lambda } \right)\hat i + \left( {2 + 2\lambda } \right)\hat j + \left( {3 + \lambda } \right)\hat k{/tex}

Again, {tex}\vec c = 3\hat i + \hat j = 3\hat i + \hat j + 0\hat k{/tex}

Since, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} therefore, {tex}\left( {\vec a + \lambda \vec b} \right).\vec c = 0{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 6 - 3\lambda + 2 + 2\lambda = 0 \Rightarrow - \lambda + 8 = 0{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow - \lambda = - 8 \Rightarrow \lambda = 8{/tex}

question_html: Write the projection of {tex}(\vec { b } + \vec { c }){/tex} on {tex}\vec { a },{/tex} where {tex}\vec { a } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } , \vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { c } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }.{/tex}
question_text: Write the projection of {tex}(\vec { b } + \vec { c }){/tex} on {tex}\vec { a },{/tex} where {tex}\vec { a } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } , \vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { c } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }.{/tex}
explanation: We have to find the projection of {tex}(\vec { b } + \vec { c }){/tex} on {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} Given that {tex}\vec { a } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } , \vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { c } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }{/tex} Consider, {tex}( \vec { b } + \vec { c } ) = ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } ) + (2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k } ){/tex} {tex}3 \hat { i} + \hat { j } + 2 \hat { k }{/tex} Now, the projection of {tex}\vec { b } + \vec { c }{/tex} on {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} is given by {tex}\frac { ( \vec { b } + \vec { c } ) \cdot \vec { a } } { | \vec { a } | } ={/tex} {tex}\frac { ( 3 \hat { i } + \hat { j } + 2 \hat { k } ) \cdot ( 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } ) } { \sqrt { 2 ^ { 2 } + ( - 2 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 1 ) ^ { 2 } } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 6 - 2 + 2 } { \sqrt { 4 + 4 + 1 } } = \frac { 6 } { 3 } = 2{/tex}
solution: We have to find the projection of {tex}(\vec { b } + \vec { c }){/tex} on {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} Given that {tex}\vec { a } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } , \vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { c } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }{/tex} Consider, {tex}( \vec { b } + \vec { c } ) = ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } ) + (2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k } ){/tex} {tex}3 \hat { i} + \hat { j } + 2 \hat { k }{/tex} Now, the projection of {tex}\vec { b } + \vec { c }{/tex} on {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} is given by {tex}\frac { ( \vec { b } + \vec { c } ) \cdot \vec { a } } { | \vec { a } | } ={/tex} {tex}\frac { ( 3 \hat { i } + \hat { j } + 2 \hat { k } ) \cdot ( 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } ) } { \sqrt { 2 ^ { 2 } + ( - 2 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 1 ) ^ { 2 } } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 6 - 2 + 2 } { \sqrt { 4 + 4 + 1 } } = \frac { 6 } { 3 } = 2{/tex}
solution_html:
We have to find the projection of {tex}(\vec { b } + \vec { c }){/tex} on  {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} 

Given that {tex}\vec { a } = 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } , \vec { b } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and
{tex}\vec { c } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }{/tex}
Consider, {tex}( \vec { b } + \vec { c } ) = ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } ) + (2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k } ){/tex}
{tex}3 \hat { i} + \hat { j } + 2 \hat { k }{/tex}
Now, the projection of {tex}\vec { b } + \vec { c }{/tex} on {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} is given by
{tex}\frac { ( \vec { b } + \vec { c } ) \cdot \vec { a } } { | \vec { a } | } ={/tex}{tex}\frac { ( 3 \hat { i } + \hat { j } + 2 \hat { k } ) \cdot ( 2 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } + \hat { k } ) } { \sqrt { 2 ^ { 2 } + ( - 2 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 1 ) ^ { 2 } } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 6 - 2 + 2 } { \sqrt { 4 + 4 + 1 } } = \frac { 6 } { 3 } = 2{/tex}

question_html: If {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } {/tex} = 400 and {tex} | \vec { b } |{/tex} = 5, then find the value of {tex} | \vec { a } |{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } {/tex} = 400 and {tex} | \vec { b } |{/tex} = 5, then find the value of {tex} | \vec { a } |{/tex} .
explanation: Given, {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a }. \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400{/tex} and {tex} | \vec { a } | = 5{/tex} Consider, {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a }. \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } \theta = 400{/tex} {tex} [ \because | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \sin \theta \text { and } | \vec { a } . \vec { b } | = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \cos \theta \|{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow d | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \left( \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + \cos ^ { 2 } \theta \right) = 400{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 25 | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400 \quad [ \because | \vec { a } | = 5 ]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow | \vec { b } | ^{ 2 } = 16 \Rightarrow | \vec { b } | = 4{/tex} [ {tex} \therefore{/tex} length cannot be negative]
solution: Given, {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a }. \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400{/tex} and {tex} | \vec { a } | = 5{/tex} Consider, {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a }. \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } \theta = 400{/tex} {tex} [ \because | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \sin \theta \text { and } | \vec { a } . \vec { b } | = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \cos \theta \|{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow d | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \left( \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + \cos ^ { 2 } \theta \right) = 400{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 25 | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400 \quad [ \because | \vec { a } | = 5 ]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow | \vec { b } | ^{ 2 } = 16 \Rightarrow | \vec { b } | = 4{/tex} [ {tex} \therefore{/tex} length cannot be negative]
solution_html:
Given, {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a }. \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400{/tex} and {tex} | \vec { a } | = 5{/tex}
Consider, {tex} | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } + | \vec { a }. \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \cos ^ { 2 } \theta = 400{/tex}
{tex} [ \because | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \sin \theta \text { and } | \vec { a } . \vec { b } | = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \cos \theta \|{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow d | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } \left( \sin ^ { 2 } \theta + \cos ^ { 2 } \theta \right) = 400{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 25 | \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 400 \quad [ \because | \vec { a } | = 5 ]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow | \vec { b } | ^{ 2 } = 16 \Rightarrow | \vec { b } | = 4{/tex}
[{tex} \therefore{/tex} length cannot be negative]

question_html: The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are {tex}2 \hat { i } - 4 \hat { j } - 5 \hat { k }{/tex}and {tex}2 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex} Find the two unit vectors parallel to its diagonals. Using the diagonal vectors, find the area of the parallelogram.
question_text: The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are {tex}2 \hat { i } - 4 \hat { j } - 5 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}2 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex} Find the two unit vectors parallel to its diagonals. Using the diagonal vectors, find the area of the parallelogram.
explanation: Let ABCD be the parallelogram. According to the question, {tex}\vec { A B } = 2 \hat { i} - 4 \hat { j } - 5 \hat { k } \text { and } \vec { A D } = 2 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j} + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex} By using parallelogram law of addition , we get The diagonal {tex}\vec { A C }{/tex} is given by {tex}\vec { A B } + \vec { A D }{/tex} {tex}= \ 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} The diagonal {tex}\vec { B D }{/tex} is given by {tex}\vec { B C } + \vec { B A }{/tex} {tex}= \vec { A D } - \vec { A B } = 6 \hat { j } + 8 \hat { k }{/tex} Now, the unit vector along {tex}\vec { A C }{/tex} is given by {tex}\frac { \vec { A C } } { | \vec { A C } | } = \frac { 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 16 + 4 + 4 } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 4 \hat {i} - 2 \hat { j} - 2 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 24 } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j} - 2 \hat { k } } { 2 \sqrt { 6 } } {/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 6 } } ( 2 \hat {i} - \hat { j } - \hat { k } ){/tex} the unit vector along {tex}\vec { B D }{/tex} is given by {tex}\frac { \vec { B D } } { | \vec { B D } | } = \frac { 6 \hat {j } + 8 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 36 + 64 } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 6 \hat {j} + 8 \hat { k } } { 10 } {/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 5 } ( 3 \hat { j} + 4 \hat { k } ){/tex} Now, area of parallelogram ABCD {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } | \vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } |{/tex} Here, {tex}\vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { \hat { i } } & { \hat { j } } & { \hat { k } } \\ { 4 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 0 } & { 6 } & { 8 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \hat { i } ( - 16 + 12 ) - \hat { j } ( 32 - 0 ) + \hat { k } ( 24 - 0 ){/tex} {tex}= - 4 \hat { i } - 32 \hat { j} + 24 \hat { k }{/tex} {tex}| \vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } | = \sqrt { ( - 4 ) ^ { 2 } + ( - 32 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 24 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}= \sqrt { 4 ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + 8 ^ { 2 } + 6 ^ { 2 } \right) }{/tex} {tex}= 4 \sqrt { 1 + 64 + 36 } {/tex} {tex}= 4 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of parallelogram ABCD = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 2 } \times 4 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex} {tex}= 2 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex} sq units.

According to the question, {tex}\vec { A B } = 2 \hat { i} - 4 \hat { j } - 5 \hat { k } \text { and } \vec { A D } = 2 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j} + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex} By using parallelogram law of addition , we get The diagonal {tex}\vec { A C }{/tex} is given by {tex}\vec { A B } + \vec { A D }{/tex} {tex}= \ 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} The diagonal {tex}\vec { B D }{/tex} is given by {tex}\vec { B C } + \vec { B A }{/tex} {tex}= \vec { A D } - \vec { A B } = 6 \hat { j } + 8 \hat { k }{/tex} Now, the unit vector along {tex}\vec { A C }{/tex} is given by {tex}\frac { \vec { A C } } { | \vec { A C } | } = \frac { 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 16 + 4 + 4 } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 4 \hat {i} - 2 \hat { j} - 2 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 24 } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j} - 2 \hat { k } } { 2 \sqrt { 6 } } {/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 6 } } ( 2 \hat {i} - \hat { j } - \hat { k } ){/tex} the unit vector along {tex}\vec { B D }{/tex} is given by {tex}\frac { \vec { B D } } { | \vec { B D } | } = \frac { 6 \hat {j } + 8 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 36 + 64 } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 6 \hat {j} + 8 \hat { k } } { 10 } {/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 5 } ( 3 \hat { j} + 4 \hat { k } ){/tex} Now, area of parallelogram ABCD {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } | \vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } |{/tex} Here, {tex}\vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { \hat { i } } & { \hat { j } } & { \hat { k } } \\ { 4 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 0 } & { 6 } & { 8 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \hat { i } ( - 16 + 12 ) - \hat { j } ( 32 - 0 ) + \hat { k } ( 24 - 0 ){/tex} {tex}= - 4 \hat { i } - 32 \hat { j} + 24 \hat { k }{/tex} {tex}| \vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } | = \sqrt { ( - 4 ) ^ { 2 } + ( - 32 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 24 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}= \sqrt { 4 ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + 8 ^ { 2 } + 6 ^ { 2 } \right) }{/tex} {tex}= 4 \sqrt { 1 + 64 + 36 } {/tex} {tex}= 4 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of parallelogram ABCD = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 2 } \times 4 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex} {tex}= 2 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex} sq units.
solution_html:
Let ABCD be the parallelogram.

According to the question,
{tex}\vec { A B } = 2 \hat { i} - 4 \hat { j } - 5 \hat { k } \text { and } \vec { A D } = 2 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j} + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex}

 By using parallelogram law of addition , we get 
The diagonal {tex}\vec { A C }{/tex} is given by {tex}\vec { A B } + \vec { A D }{/tex}{tex}= \ 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex}
The diagonal {tex}\vec { B D }{/tex} is given by {tex}\vec { B C } + \vec { B A }{/tex}{tex}= \vec { A D } - \vec { A B } = 6 \hat { j } + 8 \hat { k }{/tex}
Now, the unit vector along {tex}\vec { A C }{/tex} is given by
{tex}\frac { \vec { A C } } { | \vec { A C } | } = \frac { 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 16 + 4 + 4 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 4 \hat {i} - 2 \hat { j} - 2 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 24 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 4 \hat { i } - 2 \hat { j} - 2 \hat { k } } { 2 \sqrt { 6 } } {/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 1 } { \sqrt { 6 } } ( 2 \hat {i} - \hat { j } - \hat { k } ){/tex}
the unit vector along {tex}\vec { B D }{/tex} is given by
{tex}\frac { \vec { B D } } { | \vec { B D } | } = \frac { 6 \hat {j } + 8 \hat { k } } { \sqrt { 36 + 64 } }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 6 \hat {j} + 8 \hat { k } } { 10 } {/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 5 } ( 3 \hat { j} + 4 \hat { k } ){/tex}
Now, area of parallelogram ABCD
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } | \vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } |{/tex}
Here, {tex}\vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { \hat { i } } & { \hat { j } } & { \hat { k } } \\ { 4 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 0 } & { 6 } & { 8 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
{tex}= \hat { i } ( - 16 + 12 ) - \hat { j } ( 32 - 0 ) + \hat { k } ( 24 - 0 ){/tex}
{tex}= - 4 \hat { i } - 32 \hat { j} + 24 \hat { k }{/tex}
{tex}| \vec { A C } \times \vec { B D } | = \sqrt { ( - 4 ) ^ { 2 } + ( - 32 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 24 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \sqrt { 4 ^ { 2 } \left( 1 + 8 ^ { 2 } + 6 ^ { 2 } \right) }{/tex}
{tex}= 4 \sqrt { 1 + 64 + 36 } {/tex}

{tex}= 4 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of parallelogram ABCD = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 2 } \times 4 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex}
{tex}= 2 \sqrt { 101 }{/tex}sq units.

question_html: If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}, \vec{\mathrm{b}}=-\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}} \text { and } \vec{\mathrm{c}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}, \vec{\mathrm{b}}=-\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}} \text { and } \vec{\mathrm{c}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} are such that {tex}\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} , then find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} .
explanation: Here we have, {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}{/tex} Now, we have {tex}\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k})+\lambda(-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = {tex}(2-\lambda) \hat{i}+(2+2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(3+\lambda) \hat{k}{/tex} If {tex}(\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} , then we get {tex}(\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}[(2-\lambda) \hat{i}+(2+2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(3+\lambda) \hat{k}] \cdot(3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 - {tex}\lambda{/tex} )3 + (2 + 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} )1 + (3 + {tex}\lambda{/tex} )0 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6 - 3 {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 2 + 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 8 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 8 Hence, the required value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} is 8.
solution: Here we have, {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}{/tex} Now, we have {tex}\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k})+\lambda(-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = {tex}(2-\lambda) \hat{i}+(2+2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(3+\lambda) \hat{k}{/tex} If {tex}(\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex} , then we get {tex}(\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}[(2-\lambda) \hat{i}+(2+2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(3+\lambda) \hat{k}] \cdot(3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 - {tex}\lambda{/tex} )3 + (2 + 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} )1 + (3 + {tex}\lambda{/tex} )0 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6 - 3 {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 2 + 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} - {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 8 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 8 Hence, the required value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} is 8.
solution_html:
Here we have,  {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}{/tex} 
Now, we have
{tex}\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k})+\lambda(-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = {tex}(2-\lambda) \hat{i}+(2+2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(3+\lambda) \hat{k}{/tex} 
If {tex}(\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec c{/tex}, then we get
{tex}(\vec{a}+\lambda \vec{b}) \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}[(2-\lambda) \hat{i}+(2+2 \lambda) \hat{j}+(3+\lambda) \hat{k}] \cdot(3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}){/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (2 - {tex}\lambda{/tex})3 + (2 + 2{tex}\lambda{/tex})1 + (3 + {tex}\lambda{/tex})0 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6 - 3{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 2 + 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 8 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 8
Hence, the required value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} is 8.

question_html: if {tex}\vec{a}, \vec{b}{/tex} are two vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=| \vec{b}|{/tex} then prove that {tex}\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}
question_text: if {tex}\vec{a}, \vec{b}{/tex} are two vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=| \vec{b}|{/tex} then prove that {tex}\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}{/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}
explanation: Given that, {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=| \vec{b}|{/tex} Squaring both sides, {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|^{2}=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|^{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{a}+\vec{b})=\vec{b} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{a}+2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}} \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}+2 \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}})=0{/tex} {tex}\therefore \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \text { is perpendicular to }{ \vec{a} }{/tex}
solution: Given that, {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=| \vec{b}|{/tex} Squaring both sides, {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|^{2}=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|^{2}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{a}+\vec{b})=\vec{b} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{a}+2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}} \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}+2 \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}})=0{/tex} {tex}\therefore \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \text { is perpendicular to }{ \vec{a} }{/tex}
solution_html:
Given that, {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=| \vec{b}|{/tex}
Squaring both sides,
{tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|^{2}=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|^{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{a}+\vec{b})=\vec{b} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{a}+2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}} \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}+2 \overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}})=0{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \text { is perpendicular to }{ \vec{a} }{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} are unit vectors and {tex}\theta{/tex} is the angle between them, then prove that {tex}\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} are unit vectors and {tex}\theta{/tex} is the angle between them, then prove that {tex}\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{\left|\vec a\right|^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2-2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|\cos\theta}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{1^2+1^2-2\times1\times1\times\cos\theta}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{2-2\cos\theta}=\sqrt{2\left(1-\cos\theta\right)}{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} 1 - cos {tex}\theta{/tex} = 2 sin 2 {tex}\frac{\theta}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{2\left(2\sin^2\frac\theta2\right)}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=2\sin\frac\theta2{/tex} {tex}\frac12\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sin\frac\theta2{/tex}
solution: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{\left|\vec a\right|^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2-2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|\cos\theta}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{1^2+1^2-2\times1\times1\times\cos\theta}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{2-2\cos\theta}=\sqrt{2\left(1-\cos\theta\right)}{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} 1 - cos {tex}\theta{/tex} = 2 sin 2 {tex}\frac{\theta}{2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{2\left(2\sin^2\frac\theta2\right)}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=2\sin\frac\theta2{/tex} {tex}\frac12\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sin\frac\theta2{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\because{/tex}{tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{\left|\vec a\right|^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2-2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|\cos\theta}{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{1^2+1^2-2\times1\times1\times\cos\theta}{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{2-2\cos\theta}=\sqrt{2\left(1-\cos\theta\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} 1 - cos{tex}\theta{/tex} = 2 sin2 {tex}\frac{\theta}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sqrt{2\left(2\sin^2\frac\theta2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=2\sin\frac\theta2{/tex}
{tex}\frac12\left|\vec a-\vec b\right|=\sin\frac\theta2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} are three vectors, then find a vector perpendicular to both the vectors {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} and {tex}(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} are three vectors, then find a vector perpendicular to both the vectors {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} and {tex}(\vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} .
explanation: Given {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{c}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} Now, {tex}(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b){/tex} {tex}=\widehat i+\widehat j-2\widehat k-\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k=3\widehat j{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}(\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c)=-\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k+\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k=3\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​we know that , cross product of two vectors give the perpendicular vector to it. So, {tex}(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b)\times(\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c){/tex} {tex}=\begin{vmatrix}\widehat i&\widehat j&\widehat k\\0&3&0\\0&0&3\end{vmatrix}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\left(9-0\right)\widehat i-\left(0-0\right)\widehat j+\left(0-0\right)\widehat k{/tex} {tex}=9\widehat i{/tex} ​​​​​​​Therefore, the required perpendicular vector is {tex}9\widehat i{/tex} ​​​​​​​.
solution: Given {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{c}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} Now, {tex}(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b){/tex} {tex}=\widehat i+\widehat j-2\widehat k-\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k=3\widehat j{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}(\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c)=-\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k+\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k=3\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​we know that , cross product of two vectors give the perpendicular vector to it. So, {tex}(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b)\times(\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c){/tex} {tex}=\begin{vmatrix}\widehat i&\widehat j&\widehat k\\0&3&0\\0&0&3\end{vmatrix}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\left(9-0\right)\widehat i-\left(0-0\right)\widehat j+\left(0-0\right)\widehat k{/tex} {tex}=9\widehat i{/tex} ​​​​​​​Therefore, the required perpendicular vector is {tex}9\widehat i{/tex} ​​​​​​​.
solution_html:
Given {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex},
{tex}\vec{c}=-\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}
Now,
{tex}(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b){/tex} {tex}=\widehat i+\widehat j-2\widehat k-\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k=3\widehat j{/tex}
​​​​​​​​​​​​​​{tex}(\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c)=-\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k+\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k=3\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​we know that , cross product of two vectors give the perpendicular vector to it.
So, {tex}(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b)\times(\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c){/tex} {tex}=\begin{vmatrix}\widehat i&\widehat j&\widehat k\\0&3&0\\0&0&3\end{vmatrix}{/tex}
​​​​​​​{tex}=\left(9-0\right)\widehat i-\left(0-0\right)\widehat j+\left(0-0\right)\widehat k{/tex} {tex}=9\widehat i{/tex}
​​​​​​​Therefore, the required perpendicular vector is {tex}9\widehat i{/tex}​​​​​​​.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{\text{a}}, \vec{\text{b}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\text{c}}{/tex} are unit vectors such that {tex}\vec{\text{a}}+\vec{\text{b}}+\vec{\text{c}}=\vec{0}{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}+\vec{\text{b}} \cdot \vec{\text{c}}+\vec{\text{c}} \cdot \vec{\text{a}}{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{\text{a}}, \vec{\text{b}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\text{c}}{/tex} are unit vectors such that {tex}\vec{\text{a}}+\vec{\text{b}}+\vec{\text{c}}=\vec{0}{/tex} , then find the value of {tex}\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}+\vec{\text{b}} \cdot \vec{\text{c}}+\vec{\text{c}} \cdot \vec{\text{a}}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\because{/tex} a, b and c are unit vectors, {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|\overrightarrow c\right|=1{/tex} Now, {tex}\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow c=0(given){/tex} {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow c\right)^2=0{/tex} {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2{/tex} {tex}+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} = 0 ​​1 + 1 + 1 + 2 {tex}(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} = 0 ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} {tex}=\frac{-3}2{/tex} ​​​​​​​
solution: {tex}\because{/tex} a, b and c are unit vectors, {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|\overrightarrow c\right|=1{/tex} Now, {tex}\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow c=0(given){/tex} {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow c\right)^2=0{/tex} {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2{/tex} {tex}+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} = 0 ​​1 + 1 + 1 + 2 {tex}(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} = 0 ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} {tex}=\frac{-3}2{/tex} ​​​​​​​
solution_html:
{tex}\because{/tex} a, b and c are unit vectors,
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|\overrightarrow c\right|=1{/tex}
Now, {tex}\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow c=0(given){/tex}
{tex}\left(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow c\right)^2=0{/tex}
{tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2{/tex} {tex}+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} = 0
​​1 + 1 + 1 + 2{tex}(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} = 0
​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​{tex}(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a){/tex} {tex}=\frac{-3}2{/tex}​​​​​​​

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Let {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}. If {tex}\hat{n}{/tex} is a unit vector such that {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = 0 and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = 0, then find {tex}|\vec{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}|{/tex}.
question_text: Let {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} . If {tex}\hat{n}{/tex} is a unit vector such that {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = 0 and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = 0, then find {tex}|\vec{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}|{/tex} .
explanation: Given {tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j,\;\vec b=\hat i-\hat j,\;\vec c=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k{/tex} {tex}\hat n=\frac{\vec a\times\vec b}{\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|}{/tex} So, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&1&0\\1&-1&0\end{vmatrix}=-2\hat k{/tex} ∴ {tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{\left|c.\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)\right|}{\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{\left|\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(-2\hat k\right)\right|}{\left|-2\hat k\right|}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{2\hat k}{2\hat k}=1{/tex}
solution: Given {tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j,\;\vec b=\hat i-\hat j,\;\vec c=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k{/tex} {tex}\hat n=\frac{\vec a\times\vec b}{\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|}{/tex} So, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&1&0\\1&-1&0\end{vmatrix}=-2\hat k{/tex} ∴ {tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{\left|c.\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)\right|}{\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{\left|\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(-2\hat k\right)\right|}{\left|-2\hat k\right|}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{2\hat k}{2\hat k}=1{/tex}
solution_html:
Given {tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j,\;\vec b=\hat i-\hat j,\;\vec c=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\hat n=\frac{\vec a\times\vec b}{\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|}{/tex}
So, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&1&0\\1&-1&0\end{vmatrix}=-2\hat k{/tex}
∴ {tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{\left|c.\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)\right|}{\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|}{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{\left|\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(-2\hat k\right)\right|}{\left|-2\hat k\right|}{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{2\hat k}{2\hat k}=1{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are unit vectors inclined at an angle 30o to each other, then find the area of the parallelogram with {tex}(\vec{a}+3 \vec{b}){/tex} and {tex}(3 \vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} as adjacent sides.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are unit vectors inclined at an angle 30 o to each other, then find the area of the parallelogram with {tex}(\vec{a}+3 \vec{b}){/tex} and {tex}(3 \vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} as adjacent sides.
explanation: Area of a parallelogram having adjacent side as p and q = {tex}\left|\vec p\times\vec q\right|{/tex} Area {tex}=\left|\left(\vec a+3\vec b\right)\times\left(3\vec a+\vec b\right)\right|{/tex} {tex}=\left|3\left(\vec a\times\vec a\right)+\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)+9\left(\vec b\times\vec a\right)+3\left(\vec b\times\vec b\right)\right|{/tex} {tex}=\left|3\times0+\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)-9\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)+3\times0\right|{/tex} {tex}=\left|-8\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)\right|=8\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|{/tex} {tex}=8\left|\vec a\right|.\left|\vec b\right|\sin\theta=8\times1\times1\times\sin30^\circ{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=8\times\frac12=4\:{/tex} ​​​​​​​sq.unit​​​​​​​
solution: Area of a parallelogram having adjacent side as p and q = {tex}\left|\vec p\times\vec q\right|{/tex} Area {tex}=\left|\left(\vec a+3\vec b\right)\times\left(3\vec a+\vec b\right)\right|{/tex} {tex}=\left|3\left(\vec a\times\vec a\right)+\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)+9\left(\vec b\times\vec a\right)+3\left(\vec b\times\vec b\right)\right|{/tex} {tex}=\left|3\times0+\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)-9\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)+3\times0\right|{/tex} {tex}=\left|-8\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)\right|=8\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|{/tex} {tex}=8\left|\vec a\right|.\left|\vec b\right|\sin\theta=8\times1\times1\times\sin30^\circ{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=8\times\frac12=4\:{/tex} ​​​​​​​sq.unit​​​​​​​
solution_html:
Area of a parallelogram having adjacent side as p and q = {tex}\left|\vec p\times\vec q\right|{/tex}
Area {tex}=\left|\left(\vec a+3\vec b\right)\times\left(3\vec a+\vec b\right)\right|{/tex}
{tex}=\left|3\left(\vec a\times\vec a\right)+\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)+9\left(\vec b\times\vec a\right)+3\left(\vec b\times\vec b\right)\right|{/tex}
{tex}=\left|3\times0+\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)-9\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)+3\times0\right|{/tex}
{tex}=\left|-8\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)\right|=8\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|{/tex}
{tex}=8\left|\vec a\right|.\left|\vec b\right|\sin\theta=8\times1\times1\times\sin30^\circ{/tex}
​​​​​​​{tex}=8\times\frac12=4\:{/tex}​​​​​​​sq.unit​​​​​​​

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are two vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=|\vec{b}|{/tex}, then prove that {tex}(\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec {a}{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are two vectors such that {tex}|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|=|\vec{b}|{/tex} , then prove that {tex}(\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|{/tex} We need to prove {tex}\left(\vec a\right).\left(\vec a+2\vec b\right)=0{/tex} So, {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|{/tex} Squaring both sides {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|^2=\left|\vec b\right|^2{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a\right|^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|^2{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\vec a(\vec a+2\vec b)=0{/tex}
solution: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|{/tex} We need to prove {tex}\left(\vec a\right).\left(\vec a+2\vec b\right)=0{/tex} So, {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|{/tex} Squaring both sides {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|^2=\left|\vec b\right|^2{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a\right|^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|^2{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\vec a(\vec a+2\vec b)=0{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|{/tex}
We need to prove {tex}\left(\vec a\right).\left(\vec a+2\vec b\right)=0{/tex}
So, {tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|{/tex}
Squaring both sides
{tex}\left|\vec a+\vec b\right|^2=\left|\vec b\right|^2{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec a\right|^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|=\left|\vec b\right|^2{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec a\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|=0{/tex}
{tex}\vec a(\vec a+2\vec b)=0{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: In a parallelogram PQRS, {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{PQ}}{/tex} = {tex}3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{PS}}{/tex} = {tex}-\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}. Find {tex}|\overrightarrow{P R}|{/tex} and {tex}|\overrightarrow{Q S}|{/tex}.
question_text: In a parallelogram PQRS, {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{PQ}}{/tex} = {tex}3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{PS}}{/tex} = {tex}-\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} . Find {tex}|\overrightarrow{P R}|{/tex} and {tex}|\overrightarrow{Q S}|{/tex} .
explanation: Let {tex}\vec{PQ}=\vec a=3\hat{\mathrm i}-2\hat j+2\hat k{/tex} {tex}\vec{PS}=\vec b=-\hat{\mathrm i}-2\hat{\mathrm k}{/tex} In {tex}\triangle{/tex} PQR, using the triangle law of vector addition {tex}\vec a+\vec b=\vec{PR}{/tex} ...(1) {tex}\left(3\hat i-2\hat j+2\hat k\right)+\left(-\hat i-2\hat k\right)=\vec{PR}{/tex} {tex}\vec{PR}=2\hat i-2\hat j{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec{PR}\right|=\sqrt{4+4}=\sqrt8{/tex} In {tex}\triangle{/tex} PQS, {tex}\vec b-\vec a=\vec{QS}{/tex} {tex}\left(-\hat i-2\hat j\right)-\left(3\hat i-2\hat j+2\hat k\right)=\vec{QS}{/tex} {tex}\vec{QS}=-4\hat i-2\hat k{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec{QS}\right|=\sqrt{16+4}=\sqrt{20}{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}\vec{PQ}=\vec a=3\hat{\mathrm i}-2\hat j+2\hat k{/tex} {tex}\vec{PS}=\vec b=-\hat{\mathrm i}-2\hat{\mathrm k}{/tex} In {tex}\triangle{/tex} PQR, using the triangle law of vector addition {tex}\vec a+\vec b=\vec{PR}{/tex} ...(1) {tex}\left(3\hat i-2\hat j+2\hat k\right)+\left(-\hat i-2\hat k\right)=\vec{PR}{/tex} {tex}\vec{PR}=2\hat i-2\hat j{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec{PR}\right|=\sqrt{4+4}=\sqrt8{/tex} In {tex}\triangle{/tex} PQS, {tex}\vec b-\vec a=\vec{QS}{/tex} {tex}\left(-\hat i-2\hat j\right)-\left(3\hat i-2\hat j+2\hat k\right)=\vec{QS}{/tex} {tex}\vec{QS}=-4\hat i-2\hat k{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec{QS}\right|=\sqrt{16+4}=\sqrt{20}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\vec{PQ}=\vec a=3\hat{\mathrm i}-2\hat j+2\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\vec{PS}=\vec b=-\hat{\mathrm i}-2\hat{\mathrm k}{/tex}

In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR, using the triangle law of vector addition
{tex}\vec a+\vec b=\vec{PR}{/tex} ...(1)
{tex}\left(3\hat i-2\hat j+2\hat k\right)+\left(-\hat i-2\hat k\right)=\vec{PR}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{PR}=2\hat i-2\hat j{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec{PR}\right|=\sqrt{4+4}=\sqrt8{/tex}
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQS,
{tex}\vec b-\vec a=\vec{QS}{/tex}
{tex}\left(-\hat i-2\hat j\right)-\left(3\hat i-2\hat j+2\hat k\right)=\vec{QS}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{QS}=-4\hat i-2\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec{QS}\right|=\sqrt{16+4}=\sqrt{20}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{d}{/tex} are four non-zero vectors such that {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=4 \vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex}, then show that {tex}(\vec{a}-2 \vec{d}){/tex} is parallel to {tex}(2 \vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{d}}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{d}{/tex} are four non-zero vectors such that {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=4 \vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex} , then show that {tex}(\vec{a}-2 \vec{d}){/tex} is parallel to {tex}(2 \vec{b}-\vec{c}){/tex} where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{d}}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}} \neq 2 \vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} .
explanation: Given, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=4 \vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex} To show: {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow d\right)\times\left(2\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c\right)=0{/tex} Taking LHS {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow d\right)\times\left(2\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c\right){/tex} {tex}=(\overrightarrow a\times2\overrightarrow b)-\left(\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow c\right)-4\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow c{/tex} ​​​​​​ {tex}=2(\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow b)-\left(4\overrightarrow b\times\overrightarrow d\right)-4\overrightarrow{(d}\times\overrightarrow b)+2(\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow c){/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=2(\overrightarrow c\times\overrightarrow d)-4\left(\overrightarrow b\times\overrightarrow d\right)+4\overrightarrow{(b}\times\overrightarrow d)-2(\overrightarrow c\times\overrightarrow d){/tex} ​​​​​​​= 0 = RHS
solution: Given, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=4 \vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex} To show: {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow d\right)\times\left(2\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c\right)=0{/tex} Taking LHS {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow d\right)\times\left(2\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c\right){/tex} {tex}=(\overrightarrow a\times2\overrightarrow b)-\left(\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow c\right)-4\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow c{/tex} ​​​​​​ {tex}=2(\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow b)-\left(4\overrightarrow b\times\overrightarrow d\right)-4\overrightarrow{(d}\times\overrightarrow b)+2(\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow c){/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=2(\overrightarrow c\times\overrightarrow d)-4\left(\overrightarrow b\times\overrightarrow d\right)+4\overrightarrow{(b}\times\overrightarrow d)-2(\overrightarrow c\times\overrightarrow d){/tex} ​​​​​​​= 0 = RHS
solution_html:
Given, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \times \vec{d}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{c}=4 \vec{b} \times \vec{d}{/tex}
To show: {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow d\right)\times\left(2\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c\right)=0{/tex}
Taking LHS {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow d\right)\times\left(2\overrightarrow b-\overrightarrow c\right){/tex}
{tex}=(\overrightarrow a\times2\overrightarrow b)-\left(\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow c\right)-4\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow c{/tex}
​​​​​​{tex}=2(\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow b)-\left(4\overrightarrow b\times\overrightarrow d\right)-4\overrightarrow{(d}\times\overrightarrow b)+2(\overrightarrow d\times\overrightarrow c){/tex} ​​​​​​​
{tex}=2(\overrightarrow c\times\overrightarrow d)-4\left(\overrightarrow b\times\overrightarrow d\right)+4\overrightarrow{(b}\times\overrightarrow d)-2(\overrightarrow c\times\overrightarrow d){/tex} ​​​​​​​= 0 = RHS
