question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+y \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} are two vectors for which the vector {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to the vector {tex}(\vec{a}-\vec{b}){/tex}, then find all the possible values of y.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+y \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} are two vectors for which the vector {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} is perpendicular to the vector {tex}(\vec{a}-\vec{b}){/tex} , then find all the possible values of y.
explanation: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow a=2\widehat i+y\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+2\widehat j+3\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b=3\widehat i+\left(y+2\right)\widehat j+4\widehat k\;{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow a-\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+\left(y-2\right)\widehat j-2\widehat k\;{/tex} Now as they both are perpendicular to each other So, {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b\right).\left(\overrightarrow a-\overrightarrow b\right)=0{/tex} 3 + (y + 2)(y - 2) - 8 = 0 y 2 - 4 - 5 = 0 y 2 = 9 y = {tex}\pm3{/tex}
solution: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow a=2\widehat i+y\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+2\widehat j+3\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b=3\widehat i+\left(y+2\right)\widehat j+4\widehat k\;{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow a-\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+\left(y-2\right)\widehat j-2\widehat k\;{/tex} Now as they both are perpendicular to each other So, {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b\right).\left(\overrightarrow a-\overrightarrow b\right)=0{/tex} 3 + (y + 2)(y - 2) - 8 = 0 y 2 - 4 - 5 = 0 y 2 = 9 y = {tex}\pm3{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow a=2\widehat i+y\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+2\widehat j+3\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b=3\widehat i+\left(y+2\right)\widehat j+4\widehat k\;{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow a-\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+\left(y-2\right)\widehat j-2\widehat k\;{/tex}
Now as they both are perpendicular to each other
So, {tex}\left(\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b\right).\left(\overrightarrow a-\overrightarrow b\right)=0{/tex}
3 + (y + 2)(y - 2) - 8 = 0
y2 - 4 - 5 = 0
y2 = 9
y = {tex}\pm3{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are two unit vectors such that {tex}|2 \vec{a}+3 \vec{b}|=|3 \vec{a}-2 \vec{b}|{/tex}. Find the angle between {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}.
question_text: {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are two unit vectors such that {tex}|2 \vec{a}+3 \vec{b}|=|3 \vec{a}-2 \vec{b}|{/tex} . Find the angle between {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} .
explanation: Let {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=1{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|2\overrightarrow a+3\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|3\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}\left|2\overrightarrow a+3\overrightarrow b\right|^2=\left|3\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow b\right|^2{/tex} {tex}4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+9\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|=9\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+4\left|\overrightarrow b\right|-12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}4\times1+9\times1+12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}=9\times1+4\times1-12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}24\left|\overrightarrow a\right|\left|\overrightarrow b\right|\cos\theta=0{/tex} {tex}\cos\theta=0{/tex} {tex}\cos\theta=\cos0^\circ{/tex} {tex}\theta=0^\circ{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=1{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|2\overrightarrow a+3\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|3\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}\left|2\overrightarrow a+3\overrightarrow b\right|^2=\left|3\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow b\right|^2{/tex} {tex}4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+9\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|=9\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+4\left|\overrightarrow b\right|-12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}4\times1+9\times1+12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}=9\times1+4\times1-12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}24\left|\overrightarrow a\right|\left|\overrightarrow b\right|\cos\theta=0{/tex} {tex}\cos\theta=0{/tex} {tex}\cos\theta=\cos0^\circ{/tex} {tex}\theta=0^\circ{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=1{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|2\overrightarrow a+3\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|3\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex}
{tex}\left|2\overrightarrow a+3\overrightarrow b\right|^2=\left|3\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow b\right|^2{/tex}
{tex}4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+9\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|=9\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+4\left|\overrightarrow b\right|-12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex}
{tex}4\times1+9\times1+12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}=9\times1+4\times1-12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}24\left|\overrightarrow a\right|\left|\overrightarrow b\right|\cos\theta=0{/tex}
{tex}\cos\theta=0{/tex}
{tex}\cos\theta=\cos0^\circ{/tex}
{tex}\theta=0^\circ{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: ABCD is a parallelogram such that {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BD}}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}. Find {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}}{/tex}. Also, find the area of the parallelogram ABCD.
question_text: ABCD is a parallelogram such that {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BD}}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} . Find {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}}{/tex} . Also, find the area of the parallelogram ABCD.
explanation: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm{AC}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{BD}}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} Let {tex}\vec{AB}=\;\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec{BC}=\vec b{/tex} Now in {tex}\triangle{/tex} ABC, using vector addition law in triangle {tex}\;\vec a+\vec b=\vec{AC}{/tex} ...(1)​​​​​​ and in {tex}\triangle{/tex} DBA, {tex}\;\vec{BD}=-\vec a+\vec b=\vec b-\vec a{/tex} ...(2) ​​​​Adding eqn (1) and (2) {tex}2\vec b=\hat i+\hat j+2\hat i+\hat j+\hat k{/tex} = {tex}3\hat i+2\hat j+\hat k{/tex} ​​​​​ ​​ {tex}\vec b=\frac32\hat i+\hat j+\frac12\hat k{/tex} ​​​​​Now, subtracting eqn (2) from (1) {tex}2\vec a=\vec{AC}-\vec{BD}=-\hat i-\hat k{/tex} ​​ ​​​​​ {tex}\vec a=-\frac12\hat i-\frac12\hat k{/tex} ​ ​​​​​​ Now, the area of ABCD = {tex}\frac12\left|\vec{d_1}\times\vec{d_2}\right|{/tex} {tex}\frac12\left|\vec{d_1}\times\vec{d_2}\right|=\frac12\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&1&0\\2&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac12\left|\left[i\left(1-0\right)-j\left(1-0\right)+k\left(1-2\right)\right]\right|{/tex} ​ ​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac12\left|i-j-k\right|{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac12\sqrt{1+1+1}=\frac{\sqrt3}2{/tex} sq.unit​​​​​​​
solution: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm{AC}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{BD}}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} Let {tex}\vec{AB}=\;\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec{BC}=\vec b{/tex} Now in {tex}\triangle{/tex} ABC, using vector addition law in triangle {tex}\;\vec a+\vec b=\vec{AC}{/tex} ...(1)​​​​​​ and in {tex}\triangle{/tex} DBA, {tex}\;\vec{BD}=-\vec a+\vec b=\vec b-\vec a{/tex} ...(2) ​​​​Adding eqn (1) and (2) {tex}2\vec b=\hat i+\hat j+2\hat i+\hat j+\hat k{/tex} = {tex}3\hat i+2\hat j+\hat k{/tex} ​​​​​ ​​ {tex}\vec b=\frac32\hat i+\hat j+\frac12\hat k{/tex} ​​​​​Now, subtracting eqn (2) from (1) {tex}2\vec a=\vec{AC}-\vec{BD}=-\hat i-\hat k{/tex} ​​ ​​​​​ {tex}\vec a=-\frac12\hat i-\frac12\hat k{/tex} ​ ​​​​​​ Now, the area of ABCD = {tex}\frac12\left|\vec{d_1}\times\vec{d_2}\right|{/tex} {tex}\frac12\left|\vec{d_1}\times\vec{d_2}\right|=\frac12\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&1&0\\2&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac12\left|\left[i\left(1-0\right)-j\left(1-0\right)+k\left(1-2\right)\right]\right|{/tex} ​ ​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac12\left|i-j-k\right|{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac12\sqrt{1+1+1}=\frac{\sqrt3}2{/tex} sq.unit​​​​​​​
solution_html:
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm{AC}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{BD}}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}
Let {tex}\vec{AB}=\;\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec{BC}=\vec b{/tex}
Now in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC,
using vector addition law in triangle
{tex}\;\vec a+\vec b=\vec{AC}{/tex} ...(1)​​​​​​
and in {tex}\triangle{/tex}DBA,
{tex}\;\vec{BD}=-\vec a+\vec b=\vec b-\vec a{/tex}...(2)
​​​​Adding eqn (1) and (2)
{tex}2\vec b=\hat i+\hat j+2\hat i+\hat j+\hat k{/tex} = {tex}3\hat i+2\hat j+\hat k{/tex}​​​​​
​​ {tex}\vec b=\frac32\hat i+\hat j+\frac12\hat k{/tex}
​​​​​Now, subtracting eqn (2) from (1)
{tex}2\vec a=\vec{AC}-\vec{BD}=-\hat i-\hat k{/tex}​​
​​​​​ {tex}\vec a=-\frac12\hat i-\frac12\hat k{/tex}​
​​​​​​ Now, the area of ABCD = {tex}\frac12\left|\vec{d_1}\times\vec{d_2}\right|{/tex}
{tex}\frac12\left|\vec{d_1}\times\vec{d_2}\right|=\frac12\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&1&0\\2&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}=\frac12\left|\left[i\left(1-0\right)-j\left(1-0\right)+k\left(1-2\right)\right]\right|{/tex}​
​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac12\left|i-j-k\right|{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}=\frac12\sqrt{1+1+1}=\frac{\sqrt3}2{/tex}sq.unit​​​​​​​

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Using vectors, find the value of b if the points A(-1, -1, 2), B(2, b, 5) and C(3, 11, 6) are collinear. Also, determine the ratio in which the point B divides the line-segment AC internally.
question_text: Using vectors, find the value of b if the points A(-1, -1, 2), B(2, b, 5) and C(3, 11, 6) are collinear. Also, determine the ratio in which the point B divides the line-segment AC internally.
explanation: Given- A(-1, -1, 2), B(2, b, 5), C(3, 11, 6) {tex}\overrightarrow{AB}=\left(2+1\right)\widehat i+\left(b+1\right)\widehat j+\left(5-2\right)\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{AB}=3\widehat i+\left(b+1\right)\widehat j+3\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{AC}=\left(3+1\right)\widehat i+\left(11+1\right)\widehat j+\left(6-2\right)\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{AC}=4\widehat i+12\widehat j+4\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} A, B and C are collinear So, {tex}\overrightarrow{AB}=\lambda\;\overrightarrow{AC}{/tex} {tex}3\widehat i+\left(b+1\right)\widehat j+3\widehat k\;=\lambda\left(4\widehat i+12\widehat j+4\widehat k\right){/tex} On comparing, 3 = 4 {tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\lambda=\frac34{/tex} Also, b + 1 = 12 {tex}\lambda{/tex} b + 1 = 9 b = 8 Now, for finding ratio let B divides AB in k : 1 {tex}\overrightarrow{OA}=-\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k,\;\overrightarrow{OB}=2\widehat i+8\widehat j+5\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{OC}=3\widehat i+11\widehat j+6\widehat k{/tex} Using the section formula, {tex}\overrightarrow{OB}=\frac{k.\overrightarrow{OC}+1.\overrightarrow{OA}}{k+1}{/tex} {tex}2\widehat i+8\widehat j+5\widehat k=\frac{k.\left(3\widehat i+11\widehat j+6\widehat k\right)+\left(-\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k\right)}{k+1}{/tex} {tex}2\widehat i+8\widehat j+5\widehat k=\frac{\left(3k-1\right)\widehat i+\left(11k-1\right)\widehat j+\left(6k+1\right)\widehat k}{k+1}{/tex} Since both vectors are equal so comparing their components {tex}\frac{3k-1}{k+1}=2{/tex} ​​​​​​​ 3k -1 = 2k + 2 K = 3 ∴ ratios are 3 : 1
solution: Given- A(-1, -1, 2), B(2, b, 5), C(3, 11, 6) {tex}\overrightarrow{AB}=\left(2+1\right)\widehat i+\left(b+1\right)\widehat j+\left(5-2\right)\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{AB}=3\widehat i+\left(b+1\right)\widehat j+3\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{AC}=\left(3+1\right)\widehat i+\left(11+1\right)\widehat j+\left(6-2\right)\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{AC}=4\widehat i+12\widehat j+4\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} A, B and C are collinear So, {tex}\overrightarrow{AB}=\lambda\;\overrightarrow{AC}{/tex} {tex}3\widehat i+\left(b+1\right)\widehat j+3\widehat k\;=\lambda\left(4\widehat i+12\widehat j+4\widehat k\right){/tex} On comparing, 3 = 4 {tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\lambda=\frac34{/tex} Also, b + 1 = 12 {tex}\lambda{/tex} b + 1 = 9 b = 8 Now, for finding ratio let B divides AB in k : 1 {tex}\overrightarrow{OA}=-\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k,\;\overrightarrow{OB}=2\widehat i+8\widehat j+5\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{OC}=3\widehat i+11\widehat j+6\widehat k{/tex} Using the section formula, {tex}\overrightarrow{OB}=\frac{k.\overrightarrow{OC}+1.\overrightarrow{OA}}{k+1}{/tex} {tex}2\widehat i+8\widehat j+5\widehat k=\frac{k.\left(3\widehat i+11\widehat j+6\widehat k\right)+\left(-\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k\right)}{k+1}{/tex} {tex}2\widehat i+8\widehat j+5\widehat k=\frac{\left(3k-1\right)\widehat i+\left(11k-1\right)\widehat j+\left(6k+1\right)\widehat k}{k+1}{/tex} Since both vectors are equal so comparing their components {tex}\frac{3k-1}{k+1}=2{/tex} ​​​​​​​ 3k -1 = 2k + 2 K = 3 ∴ ratios are 3 : 1
solution_html:
Given- A(-1, -1, 2), B(2, b, 5), C(3, 11, 6)
{tex}\overrightarrow{AB}=\left(2+1\right)\widehat i+\left(b+1\right)\widehat j+\left(5-2\right)\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{AB}=3\widehat i+\left(b+1\right)\widehat j+3\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{AC}=\left(3+1\right)\widehat i+\left(11+1\right)\widehat j+\left(6-2\right)\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{AC}=4\widehat i+12\widehat j+4\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} A, B and C are collinear
So, {tex}\overrightarrow{AB}=\lambda\;\overrightarrow{AC}{/tex}
{tex}3\widehat i+\left(b+1\right)\widehat j+3\widehat k\;=\lambda\left(4\widehat i+12\widehat j+4\widehat k\right){/tex}
On comparing,
3 = 4{tex}\lambda{/tex}
{tex}\lambda=\frac34{/tex}
Also, b + 1 = 12{tex}\lambda{/tex}
b + 1 = 9
b = 8
Now, for finding ratio let B divides AB in k : 1
{tex}\overrightarrow{OA}=-\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k,\;\overrightarrow{OB}=2\widehat i+8\widehat j+5\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{OC}=3\widehat i+11\widehat j+6\widehat k{/tex}
Using the section formula,
{tex}\overrightarrow{OB}=\frac{k.\overrightarrow{OC}+1.\overrightarrow{OA}}{k+1}{/tex}
{tex}2\widehat i+8\widehat j+5\widehat k=\frac{k.\left(3\widehat i+11\widehat j+6\widehat k\right)+\left(-\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k\right)}{k+1}{/tex}
{tex}2\widehat i+8\widehat j+5\widehat k=\frac{\left(3k-1\right)\widehat i+\left(11k-1\right)\widehat j+\left(6k+1\right)\widehat k}{k+1}{/tex}
Since both vectors are equal so comparing their components
{tex}\frac{3k-1}{k+1}=2{/tex}​​​​​​​
3k -1 = 2k + 2
K = 3
∴ ratios are 3 : 1

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} are two vectors such that {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex}, then find the vector {tex}\vec {c}{/tex}, given that {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \vec{\mathrm{c}}=\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} = 4.
question_text: If {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} are two vectors such that {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex} , then find the vector {tex}\vec {c}{/tex} , given that {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \vec{\mathrm{c}}=\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} = 4.
explanation: Given {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex} Also {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \vec{\mathrm{c}}=\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{c}}=4{/tex} Let {tex}\vec{c}=x \hat{i}+y\hat{j}+z \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm a}\times\vec{\mathrm c}=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&-1&1\\x&y&z\end{vmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm a}\times\vec{\mathrm c}=(-z-y)\widehat i-\left(z-x\right)\widehat j+\left(y+x\right)\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \vec{\mathrm{c}}=\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}(-z-y)\widehat i-\left(z-x\right)\widehat j+\left(y+x\right)\widehat k=2\widehat i-\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​ on compairing (-z - y) = 2, z - x = 1, y + x = -3 ...(i) Also, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{c}}=4{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\left(\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k\right)\left(x\widehat i+y\widehat j+z\widehat k\right)=4{/tex} ​​​​​​​ x - y + z = 4 x - y + 1+ x = 4 ( from eqn (i)) 2x - y = 3 ...(ii) x + y = -3 (from eqn (ii)) Adding them 3x = 0 x = 0 Putting in eqn (i) y = -3, z = 1 ∴ {tex}\vec c=-3\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​
solution: Given {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex} Also {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \vec{\mathrm{c}}=\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{c}}=4{/tex} Let {tex}\vec{c}=x \hat{i}+y\hat{j}+z \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm a}\times\vec{\mathrm c}=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&-1&1\\x&y&z\end{vmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm a}\times\vec{\mathrm c}=(-z-y)\widehat i-\left(z-x\right)\widehat j+\left(y+x\right)\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \vec{\mathrm{c}}=\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}(-z-y)\widehat i-\left(z-x\right)\widehat j+\left(y+x\right)\widehat k=2\widehat i-\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​ on compairing (-z - y) = 2, z - x = 1, y + x = -3 ...(i) Also, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{c}}=4{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\left(\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k\right)\left(x\widehat i+y\widehat j+z\widehat k\right)=4{/tex} ​​​​​​​ x - y + z = 4 x - y + 1+ x = 4 ( from eqn (i)) 2x - y = 3 ...(ii) x + y = -3 (from eqn (ii)) Adding them 3x = 0 x = 0 Putting in eqn (i) y = -3, z = 1 ∴ {tex}\vec c=-3\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​
solution_html:
Given {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex}
Also {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \vec{\mathrm{c}}=\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{c}}=4{/tex}
Let {tex}\vec{c}=x \hat{i}+y\hat{j}+z \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{\mathrm a}\times\vec{\mathrm c}=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&-1&1\\x&y&z\end{vmatrix}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{\mathrm a}\times\vec{\mathrm c}=(-z-y)\widehat i-\left(z-x\right)\widehat j+\left(y+x\right)\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​{tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \vec{\mathrm{c}}=\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}(-z-y)\widehat i-\left(z-x\right)\widehat j+\left(y+x\right)\widehat k=2\widehat i-\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex}​​​​​​​ on compairing
(-z - y) = 2, z - x = 1, y + x = -3 ...(i)
Also, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \vec{\mathrm{c}}=4{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\left(\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k\right)\left(x\widehat i+y\widehat j+z\widehat k\right)=4{/tex}​​​​​​​
x - y + z = 4
x - y + 1+ x = 4 ( from eqn (i))
2x - y = 3 ...(ii)
x + y = -3 (from eqn (ii))
Adding them
3x = 0
x = 0
Putting in eqn (i)
y = -3, z = 1
∴ {tex}\vec c=-3\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex}​​​​​​​

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Write the projection of the vector {tex}(\vec{b}+\vec{c}){/tex} on the vector {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}.
question_text: Write the projection of the vector {tex}(\vec{b}+\vec{c}){/tex} on the vector {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} , where {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec a=2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\overrightarrow k{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+2\widehat j-2\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=2\widehat i-\widehat j+4\overrightarrow k\;{/tex} {tex}\left(\vec b+\vec c\right)=3\hat i+\hat j+2\vec k\;{/tex} Now, projection of (b+c) on a = {tex}\frac{\left(\vec b+\vec c\right).\vec a}{\left|\vec a\right|}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\left(3\widehat i+\widehat j+2\widehat k\right)\left(2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k\right)}{\sqrt{4+1+1}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{6-2+2}{\sqrt9}=\frac63=2{/tex}
solution: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec a=2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\overrightarrow k{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+2\widehat j-2\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=2\widehat i-\widehat j+4\overrightarrow k\;{/tex} {tex}\left(\vec b+\vec c\right)=3\hat i+\hat j+2\vec k\;{/tex} Now, projection of (b+c) on a = {tex}\frac{\left(\vec b+\vec c\right).\vec a}{\left|\vec a\right|}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\left(3\widehat i+\widehat j+2\widehat k\right)\left(2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k\right)}{\sqrt{4+1+1}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{6-2+2}{\sqrt9}=\frac63=2{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec a=2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\overrightarrow k{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i+2\widehat j-2\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow c=2\widehat i-\widehat j+4\overrightarrow k\;{/tex}
{tex}\left(\vec b+\vec c\right)=3\hat i+\hat j+2\vec k\;{/tex}
Now, projection of (b+c) on a = {tex}\frac{\left(\vec b+\vec c\right).\vec a}{\left|\vec a\right|}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\left(3\widehat i+\widehat j+2\widehat k\right)\left(2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k\right)}{\sqrt{4+1+1}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{6-2+2}{\sqrt9}=\frac63=2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are represented by vectors {tex}2 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}. Find the unit vector parallel to one of its diagonals. Also, find the area of the parallelogram.
question_text: The two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are represented by vectors {tex}2 \hat{i}-4 \hat{j}+5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} . Find the unit vector parallel to one of its diagonals. Also, find the area of the parallelogram.
explanation: Let {tex}\overrightarrow a=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex} , {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat k+\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\overrightarrow{\left|c\right|}=\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2+\left(6\right)^2+\left(2\right)^2}=\sqrt{49}=7{/tex} ​​​​​​​ Unit vector {tex}\widehat c=\frac{\overrightarrow c}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|}=\frac17\;\left(3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k\right){/tex} ​​​​​​​ ar(ABCD) = {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\2&-4&5\\1&-2&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\left|\left(12+10\right)\widehat i-\left(-6-6\right)\widehat j+\left(-4+4\right)\widehat k\right|{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\left|22\widehat i+12\widehat j\right|=\sqrt{\left(22\right)^2+\left(12\right)^2}{/tex} ​​​​​​ ar (ABCD) = {tex}\sqrt{484+144} = \sqrt{628}{/tex} = 25.05 sq unit
solution: Let {tex}\overrightarrow a=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex} , {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat k+\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow c=3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\overrightarrow{\left|c\right|}=\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2+\left(6\right)^2+\left(2\right)^2}=\sqrt{49}=7{/tex} ​​​​​​​ Unit vector {tex}\widehat c=\frac{\overrightarrow c}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|}=\frac17\;\left(3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k\right){/tex} ​​​​​​​ ar(ABCD) = {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\2&-4&5\\1&-2&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\left|\left(12+10\right)\widehat i-\left(-6-6\right)\widehat j+\left(-4+4\right)\widehat k\right|{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=\left|22\widehat i+12\widehat j\right|=\sqrt{\left(22\right)^2+\left(12\right)^2}{/tex} ​​​​​​ ar (ABCD) = {tex}\sqrt{484+144} = \sqrt{628}{/tex} = 25.05 sq unit
solution_html:
Let {tex}\overrightarrow a=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex}, {tex}\overrightarrow b=\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat{\mathrm k}{/tex}

{tex}\overrightarrow c=\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow c=2\widehat i-4\widehat j+5\widehat k+\widehat i-2\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow c=3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\overrightarrow{\left|c\right|}=\sqrt{\left(3\right)^2+\left(6\right)^2+\left(2\right)^2}=\sqrt{49}=7{/tex}​​​​​​​
Unit vector {tex}\widehat c=\frac{\overrightarrow c}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|}=\frac17\;\left(3\widehat i-6\widehat j+2\widehat k\right){/tex}​​​​​​​
ar(ABCD) = {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\times\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\2&-4&5\\1&-2&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}=\left|\left(12+10\right)\widehat i-\left(-6-6\right)\widehat j+\left(-4+4\right)\widehat k\right|{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}=\left|22\widehat i+12\widehat j\right|=\sqrt{\left(22\right)^2+\left(12\right)^2}{/tex}​​​​​​
ar (ABCD) = {tex}\sqrt{484+144} = \sqrt{628}{/tex} = 25.05 sq unit

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} are three vectors such that {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{a} \times \vec{c}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{a} \neq 0{/tex} then show that {tex}\vec{b}=\vec{c}{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} are three vectors such that {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{a} \times \vec{c}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{a} \neq 0{/tex} then show that {tex}\vec{b}=\vec{c}{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\vec a\cdot\vec b-\vec a\cdot\vec c=0{/tex} {tex}\vec a\cdot\left(\vec b-\vec c\right)=0{/tex} ​ {tex}\left|\vec a\right|.\left|\left(\vec b-\vec c\right)\right|.\cos\theta=0{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a\right|\neq0{/tex} and {tex}\cos\theta\neq0{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\vec b-\vec c=0{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\overrightarrow b=\overrightarrow c{/tex} ​​​​​​​
solution: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\vec a\cdot\vec b-\vec a\cdot\vec c=0{/tex} {tex}\vec a\cdot\left(\vec b-\vec c\right)=0{/tex} ​ {tex}\left|\vec a\right|.\left|\left(\vec b-\vec c\right)\right|.\cos\theta=0{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec a\right|\neq0{/tex} and {tex}\cos\theta\neq0{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\vec b-\vec c=0{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\overrightarrow b=\overrightarrow c{/tex} ​​​​​​​
solution_html:
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}{/tex}
{tex}\vec a\cdot\vec b-\vec a\cdot\vec c=0{/tex}
{tex}\vec a\cdot\left(\vec b-\vec c\right)=0{/tex}​
{tex}\left|\vec a\right|.\left|\left(\vec b-\vec c\right)\right|.\cos\theta=0{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec a\right|\neq0{/tex} and {tex}\cos\theta\neq0{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\vec b-\vec c=0{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\overrightarrow b=\overrightarrow c{/tex}​​​​​​​

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}|\vec{a}|{/tex} = 3, {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex} = 5, {tex}|\vec{c}|{/tex} = 4 and {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}){/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}|\vec{a}|{/tex} = 3, {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex} = 5, {tex}|\vec{c}|{/tex} = 4 and {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex} , then find the value of {tex}(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}+\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}+\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}){/tex} .
explanation: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left(\vec a+\vec b+\vec c\right)^2=\vec0{/tex} (squaring both sides) {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a)=0{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}3^2+5^2+4^2+2(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a)=0{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}2(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a){/tex} = -50​​​​​​​ {tex}(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a){/tex} = -25​​​​​​​
solution: {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left(\vec a+\vec b+\vec c\right)^2=\vec0{/tex} (squaring both sides) {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a)=0{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}3^2+5^2+4^2+2(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a)=0{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}2(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a){/tex} = -50​​​​​​​ {tex}(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a){/tex} = -25​​​​​​​
solution_html:
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\vec{0}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left(\vec a+\vec b+\vec c\right)^2=\vec0{/tex} (squaring both sides)
{tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a)=0{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}3^2+5^2+4^2+2(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a)=0{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}2(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a){/tex} = -50​​​​​​​
{tex}(\vec a\cdot\vec b+\vec b\cdot\vec c+\vec c\cdot\vec a){/tex} = -25​​​​​​​

question_html: Find all the possible vectors of magnitude {tex}5 \sqrt{3}{/tex} which are equally inclined to the coordinate axes.
question_text: Find all the possible vectors of magnitude {tex}5 \sqrt{3}{/tex} which are equally inclined to the coordinate axes.
explanation: Direction vector equally inclined to coordinate axis = {tex}\frac{\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k}{\sqrt3}{/tex} Vector = {tex}5\sqrt3\times\frac{\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k}{\sqrt3}{/tex} Vector = {tex}5\widehat i+5\widehat j+5\widehat k{/tex}
solution: Direction vector equally inclined to coordinate axis = {tex}\frac{\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k}{\sqrt3}{/tex} Vector = {tex}5\sqrt3\times\frac{\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k}{\sqrt3}{/tex} Vector = {tex}5\widehat i+5\widehat j+5\widehat k{/tex}
solution_html:
Direction vector equally inclined to coordinate axis = {tex}\frac{\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k}{\sqrt3}{/tex}
Vector = {tex}5\sqrt3\times\frac{\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k}{\sqrt3}{/tex}
Vector = {tex}5\widehat i+5\widehat j+5\widehat k{/tex}

question_html: If {tex}\vec {a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec {b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex} are mutually perpendicular vectors of equal magnitude, then prove that the vector {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex} is equally inclined to both {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex}. Also, find the angle between {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} and {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} , {tex}\vec {b}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex} are mutually perpendicular vectors of equal magnitude, then prove that the vector {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex} is equally inclined to both {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {c}{/tex} . Also, find the angle between {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} and {tex}(2 \vec{a}+\vec{b}+2 \vec{c}){/tex} .
explanation: Let {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|\overrightarrow c\right|=x{/tex} ​​Now, let angle between {tex}\overrightarrow aand\;\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right){/tex} be {tex}\theta_1{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{\overrightarrow a.\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow a\right|.\left|2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right|}{/tex} ​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{2\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\overrightarrow a\overrightarrow{.b}+2\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow c}{\left|\overrightarrow a\right|.\sqrt{4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a)}}{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{2x^2+0+0}{x.\sqrt{4x^2+x^2+x^2+0}}{/tex} {tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{2x}{\sqrt6\;x}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac2{\sqrt6}{/tex} ...(i)​​​​​​​ Now, let angle between {tex}\overrightarrow cand\;\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right){/tex} be ​​​​​​​ {tex}\theta_2{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{\overrightarrow c.\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|.\left|2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right|}{/tex} {tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{2\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow c\overrightarrow{.b}+2\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|.\sqrt{4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a)}}{/tex} c\right|^2+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a)}}{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}\overrightarrow cand\;\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right){/tex} be ​​​​​​​ {tex}\theta_2{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{\overrightarrow c.\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|.\left|2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right|}{/tex} a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a)}}{/tex} solution_html:
Let {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|\overrightarrow c\right|=x{/tex}
​​Now, let angle between {tex}\overrightarrow aand\;\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right){/tex}be {tex}\theta_1{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{\overrightarrow a.\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow a\right|.\left|2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right|}{/tex}
​​​​{tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{2\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\overrightarrow a\overrightarrow{.b}+2\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow c}{\left|\overrightarrow a\right|.\sqrt{4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a)}}{/tex}​​​​​​​​​​​​​{tex}\cos\theta_1=\frac{2x^2+0+0}{x.\sqrt{4x^2+x^2+x^2+0}}{/tex}

Now, let angle between {tex}\overrightarrow cand\;\left(2\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow b+2\overrightarrow c\right){/tex}be ​​​​​​​{tex}\theta_2{/tex}​​​​​​​

{tex}\cos\theta_2=\frac{2\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a+\overrightarrow c\overrightarrow{.b}+2\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2}{\left|\overrightarrow c\right|.\sqrt{4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+\left|\overrightarrow c\right|^2+2(\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b+\overrightarrow b.\overrightarrow c+\overrightarrow c.\overrightarrow a)}}{/tex}​​​​​​​

So, from eqn (i) and (ii) we can say given vector is equally inclined with a and c and angle between them is {tex}\frac2{\sqrt6}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {b}{/tex} are two vectors of equal magnitude and {tex}\alpha {/tex} is the angle between them, then prove that {tex}\frac{|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|}{|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|}=\cot \left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right){/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {b}{/tex} are two vectors of equal magnitude and {tex}\alpha {/tex} is the angle between them, then prove that {tex}\frac{|\vec{a}+\vec{b}|}{|\vec{a}-\vec{b}|}=\cot \left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right){/tex} .
explanation: Let {tex}\left|\vec a\right|=\left|\vec b\right|=x{/tex} Now, {tex}\frac{\vert\vec a+\vec b\vert^2}{\vert\vec a-\vec b\vert^2}{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac{\vert\vec a\vert^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|\cos\alpha}{\vert\vec a\vert^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2-2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|\cos\alpha}{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac{x^2+x^2+2x^2\cos\alpha}{x^2+x^2-2x^2\cos\alpha}{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac{2x^2+2x^2\cos\alpha}{2x^2-2x^2\cos\alpha}=\frac{2x^2\left(1+\cos\alpha\right)}{2x^2\left(1-\cos\alpha\right)}{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ Now, since 1 + cos A = 2 {tex}cos^2\frac A2{/tex} , 1- cosA = 2 {tex}sin^2\frac A2{/tex} ​​​​​​​ ∴ {tex}=\frac{2\cos^2{\frac\alpha2}}{2\sin^2{\frac\alpha2}}=cot^2\frac\alpha2{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{\vert\vec a+\vec b\vert^2}{\vert\vec a-\vec b\vert^2}=cot^2\frac\alpha2{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{\vert\vec a+\vec b\vert}{\vert\vec a-\vec b\vert}=cot\frac\alpha2{/tex} ​​​​​​​
solution: Let {tex}\left|\vec a\right|=\left|\vec b\right|=x{/tex} Now, {tex}\frac{\vert\vec a+\vec b\vert^2}{\vert\vec a-\vec b\vert^2}{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac{\vert\vec a\vert^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|\cos\alpha}{\vert\vec a\vert^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2-2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|\cos\alpha}{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac{x^2+x^2+2x^2\cos\alpha}{x^2+x^2-2x^2\cos\alpha}{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}=\frac{2x^2+2x^2\cos\alpha}{2x^2-2x^2\cos\alpha}=\frac{2x^2\left(1+\cos\alpha\right)}{2x^2\left(1-\cos\alpha\right)}{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ Now, since 1 + cos A = 2 {tex}cos^2\frac A2{/tex} , 1- cosA = 2 {tex}sin^2\frac A2{/tex} ​​​​​​​ ∴ {tex}=\frac{2\cos^2{\frac\alpha2}}{2\sin^2{\frac\alpha2}}=cot^2\frac\alpha2{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{\vert\vec a+\vec b\vert^2}{\vert\vec a-\vec b\vert^2}=cot^2\frac\alpha2{/tex} ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{\vert\vec a+\vec b\vert}{\vert\vec a-\vec b\vert}=cot\frac\alpha2{/tex} ​​​​​​​
solution_html:
Let {tex}\left|\vec a\right|=\left|\vec b\right|=x{/tex}
Now, {tex}\frac{\vert\vec a+\vec b\vert^2}{\vert\vec a-\vec b\vert^2}{/tex}​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
{tex}=\frac{\vert\vec a\vert^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2+2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|\cos\alpha}{\vert\vec a\vert^2+\left|\vec b\right|^2-2\left|\vec a\right|\left|\vec b\right|\cos\alpha}{/tex}​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
{tex}=\frac{x^2+x^2+2x^2\cos\alpha}{x^2+x^2-2x^2\cos\alpha}{/tex}​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
{tex}=\frac{2x^2+2x^2\cos\alpha}{2x^2-2x^2\cos\alpha}=\frac{2x^2\left(1+\cos\alpha\right)}{2x^2\left(1-\cos\alpha\right)}{/tex}​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
Now, since 1 + cos A = 2 {tex}cos^2\frac A2{/tex}, 1- cosA = 2{tex}sin^2\frac A2{/tex}​​​​​​​
∴ {tex}=\frac{2\cos^2{\frac\alpha2}}{2\sin^2{\frac\alpha2}}=cot^2\frac\alpha2{/tex}​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
{tex}\frac{\vert\vec a+\vec b\vert^2}{\vert\vec a-\vec b\vert^2}=cot^2\frac\alpha2{/tex}​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
{tex}\frac{\vert\vec a+\vec b\vert}{\vert\vec a-\vec b\vert}=cot\frac\alpha2{/tex}​​​​​​​

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=\hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} and the projection of vector {tex}\vec{c}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} on vector {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} is {tex}2 \sqrt{6}{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=\hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} and the projection of vector {tex}\vec{c}+\lambda \vec{b}{/tex} on vector {tex}\vec {a}{/tex} is {tex}2 \sqrt{6}{/tex} , then find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} .
explanation: Let {tex}\overrightarrow r=\overrightarrow c+\lambda\overrightarrow b{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow r=\widehat i+3\widehat j-\widehat k+\lambda(\widehat i+\widehat j-2\widehat k){/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow r=\left(1+\lambda\right)\widehat i+\left(3+\lambda\right)\widehat j-\left(1+2\lambda\right)\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​ Projection of r on a = 2√6 {tex}\frac{\overrightarrow r.\overrightarrow a}{\left|\overrightarrow a\right|}=2\sqrt6{/tex} ​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{\left[\left(1+\lambda\right)\widehat i+\left(3+\lambda\right)\widehat j-\left(1+2\lambda\right)\widehat k\right].(2\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k)}{\sqrt{4+1+1}}=2\sqrt6{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\left(2+2\lambda\right)-\left(3+\lambda\right)-\left(1+2\lambda\right)=12{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}-\lambda-2{/tex} = 12​​​​​​​ {tex}\lambda{/tex} = -14​​​​​​​
solution: Let {tex}\overrightarrow r=\overrightarrow c+\lambda\overrightarrow b{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow r=\widehat i+3\widehat j-\widehat k+\lambda(\widehat i+\widehat j-2\widehat k){/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow r=\left(1+\lambda\right)\widehat i+\left(3+\lambda\right)\widehat j-\left(1+2\lambda\right)\widehat k{/tex} ​​​​​​​ Projection of r on a = 2√6 {tex}\frac{\overrightarrow r.\overrightarrow a}{\left|\overrightarrow a\right|}=2\sqrt6{/tex} ​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{\left[\left(1+\lambda\right)\widehat i+\left(3+\lambda\right)\widehat j-\left(1+2\lambda\right)\widehat k\right].(2\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k)}{\sqrt{4+1+1}}=2\sqrt6{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\left(2+2\lambda\right)-\left(3+\lambda\right)-\left(1+2\lambda\right)=12{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}-\lambda-2{/tex} = 12​​​​​​​ {tex}\lambda{/tex} = -14​​​​​​​
solution_html:
Let {tex}\overrightarrow r=\overrightarrow c+\lambda\overrightarrow b{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow r=\widehat i+3\widehat j-\widehat k+\lambda(\widehat i+\widehat j-2\widehat k){/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow r=\left(1+\lambda\right)\widehat i+\left(3+\lambda\right)\widehat j-\left(1+2\lambda\right)\widehat k{/tex}​​​​​​​
Projection of r on a = 2√6
{tex}\frac{\overrightarrow r.\overrightarrow a}{\left|\overrightarrow a\right|}=2\sqrt6{/tex} ​​​​​​
{tex}\frac{\left[\left(1+\lambda\right)\widehat i+\left(3+\lambda\right)\widehat j-\left(1+2\lambda\right)\widehat k\right].(2\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k)}{\sqrt{4+1+1}}=2\sqrt6{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\left(2+2\lambda\right)-\left(3+\lambda\right)-\left(1+2\lambda\right)=12{/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}-\lambda-2{/tex} = 12​​​​​​​
{tex}\lambda{/tex} = -14​​​​​​​

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 1 and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}, then find {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex}
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 1 and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} , then find {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex}
explanation: {tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k,\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex} {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} Let {tex}\vec b=x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k{/tex} As, {tex}\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex} {tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=1{/tex} x + y + z = 1 ...(i) Also, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} {tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right)\times\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} {tex}\left(z-y\right)\hat i-\left(z-x\right)\hat j+\left(y-x\right)\hat k=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} On compairing z - y = 0, x - z = 1, y - x = -1 ...(ii) z = y Putting in eqn (i) x + 2y = 1 -x + y = -1 (from eqn (ii)) y = 0, z = 0, x = 1 So, {tex}\vec b=\hat i{/tex} {tex}\vec{\left|b\right|}=\sqrt1=\;1{/tex}
solution: {tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k,\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex} {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} Let {tex}\vec b=x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k{/tex} As, {tex}\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex} {tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=1{/tex} x + y + z = 1 ...(i) Also, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} {tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right)\times\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} {tex}\left(z-y\right)\hat i-\left(z-x\right)\hat j+\left(y-x\right)\hat k=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} On compairing z - y = 0, x - z = 1, y - x = -1 ...(ii) z = y Putting in eqn (i) x + 2y = 1 -x + y = -1 (from eqn (ii)) y = 0, z = 0, x = 1 So, {tex}\vec b=\hat i{/tex} {tex}\vec{\left|b\right|}=\sqrt1=\;1{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k,\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex}
{tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
Let {tex}\vec b=x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k{/tex}
As, {tex}\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex}
{tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=1{/tex}
x + y + z = 1 ...(i)
Also, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right)\times\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\left(z-y\right)\hat i-\left(z-x\right)\hat j+\left(y-x\right)\hat k=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
On compairing
z - y = 0, x - z = 1, y - x = -1 ...(ii)
z = y
Putting in eqn (i)
x + 2y = 1
-x + y = -1 (from eqn (ii))
y = 0, z = 0, x = 1
So, {tex}\vec b=\hat i{/tex}
{tex}\vec{\left|b\right|}=\sqrt1=\;1{/tex}

question_text: Write the projection of the vector {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+12 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} on (i) x-axis, and (ii) y-axis.
explanation: Projection of the {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}{/tex} on the x-axis = 3. Projection of the {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}{/tex} on the y-axis = 4.
solution: Projection of the {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}{/tex} on the x-axis = 3. Projection of the {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}{/tex} on the y-axis = 4.
solution_html:
1. Projection of the {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}{/tex} on the x-axis = 3.
  2. Projection of the {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}{/tex} on the y-axis = 4.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} are three mutually perpendicular unit vectors, find the value of {tex}|\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}+3 \vec{c}|{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} are three mutually perpendicular unit vectors, find the value of {tex}|\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}+3 \vec{c}|{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}|=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|{/tex} = 1 and {tex} \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} = {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} = 0 {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+3 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|^2{/tex} = {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}^2+4 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}^2+9 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}^2{/tex} = 1 + 4 + 9 = 14 {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}+3 \vec{c}|{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{14}{/tex}
solution: {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}|=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|{/tex} = 1 and {tex} \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} = {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} = 0 {tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+3 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|^2{/tex} = {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}^2+4 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}^2+9 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}^2{/tex} = 1 + 4 + 9 = 14 {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}+3 \vec{c}|{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{14}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}|=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|{/tex} = 1 and {tex} \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}{/tex} = {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} = 0
{tex}|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+3 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|^2{/tex} =  {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}^2+4 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}^2+9 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}^2{/tex} = 1 + 4 + 9 = 14
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}+2 \vec{b}+3 \vec{c}|{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{14}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If the sides AB and BC of a parallelogram ABCD are represented as vectors {tex}\vec{\mathrm{AB}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{BC}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}, then find the unit vector along diagonal AC.
question_text: If the sides AB and BC of a parallelogram ABCD are represented as vectors {tex}\vec{\mathrm{AB}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{BC}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} , then find the unit vector along diagonal AC.
explanation: {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}={/tex} {tex}(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} + {tex}(\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}={/tex} {tex}3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+46-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} Unit vector along AC = {tex}\frac{3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}}{|3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}|}={/tex} {tex}\frac{3}{7} \hat{i}+\frac{6}{7} \hat{j}-\frac{2}{7} \hat{k}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}={/tex} {tex}(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} + {tex}(\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}={/tex} {tex}3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+46-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} Unit vector along AC = {tex}\frac{3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}}{|3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}|}={/tex} {tex}\frac{3}{7} \hat{i}+\frac{6}{7} \hat{j}-\frac{2}{7} \hat{k}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}}={/tex} {tex}(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} + {tex}(\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}={/tex} {tex}3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+46-2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}
Unit vector along AC = {tex}\frac{3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}}{|3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}|}={/tex} {tex}\frac{3}{7} \hat{i}+\frac{6}{7} \hat{j}-\frac{2}{7} \hat{k}{/tex}

question_html: Find the angle between the vectors {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}.
question_text: Find the angle between the vectors {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} .

question_html: Using vectors, find the area of the {tex}\Delta A B C,{/tex} whose vertices are A (1, 2, 3), B(2, -1, 4) and C(4, 5, -1).
question_text: Using vectors, find the area of the {tex}\Delta A B C,{/tex} whose vertices are A (1, 2, 3), B(2, -1, 4) and C(4, 5, -1).
explanation: Given , ΔABC, whose vertices are A (1, 2, 3), B(2, -1, 4) and C(4, 5, -1). Let the position vectors of the vertices A, B and C of {tex}\Delta A B C{/tex} is {tex}\vec { O A } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + \hat { 3 k }{/tex} {tex} \vec { O B } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { O C } = 4 \hat { i } + 5 \hat { j } - \hat { k }{/tex} We know that , {tex}\vec { A B } = \vec { O B } - \vec { O A }{/tex} {tex}= ( 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k } ) - ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ){/tex} {tex}= \hat { i } - 3 \hat { j } + \hat { k }{/tex} also , {tex}\vec { A C } = \vec { O C } - \vec { O A }{/tex} {tex}= ( 4 \hat { i } + 5 \hat { j } - \hat { k } ) - ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ){/tex} {tex}= ( 3 \hat { i } + 3 \hat { j } - 4 \hat { k } ){/tex} Now, {tex}\vec { A B } \times \vec { A C } = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { \hat { i } } & { \hat { j } } & { \hat { k } } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } & { 1 } \\ { 3 } & { 3 } & { - 4 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \hat { i } ( 12 - 3 ) - \hat { j } ( - 4 - 3 ) + \hat { k } ( 3 + 9 ){/tex} {tex}= 9 \hat { i } + 7 \hat { j } + 12 \hat { k }{/tex} {tex}| \vec { A B } \times \vec { A C } | = \sqrt { ( 9 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 7 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 12 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}= \sqrt { 81 + 49 + 144 }{/tex} {tex} = \sqrt { 274 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\Delta A B C = \frac { 1 } { 2 } | \vec { A B } \times \vec { A C } |{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \sqrt { 274 }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { \sqrt { 274 } } { 2 } {/tex} {tex}\therefore \ Area = \frac { \sqrt { 274 } } { 2 } {/tex} sq units.
solution: Given , ΔABC, whose vertices are A (1, 2, 3), B(2, -1, 4) and C(4, 5, -1). Let the position vectors of the vertices A, B and C of {tex}\Delta A B C{/tex} is {tex}\vec { O A } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + \hat { 3 k }{/tex} {tex} \vec { O B } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { O C } = 4 \hat { i } + 5 \hat { j } - \hat { k }{/tex} We know that , {tex}\vec { A B } = \vec { O B } - \vec { O A }{/tex} {tex}= ( 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k } ) - ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ){/tex} {tex}= \hat { i } - 3 \hat { j } + \hat { k }{/tex} also , {tex}\vec { A C } = \vec { O C } - \vec { O A }{/tex} {tex}= ( 4 \hat { i } + 5 \hat { j } - \hat { k } ) - ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ){/tex} {tex}= ( 3 \hat { i } + 3 \hat { j } - 4 \hat { k } ){/tex} Now, {tex}\vec { A B } \times \vec { A C } = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { \hat { i } } & { \hat { j } } & { \hat { k } } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } & { 1 } \\ { 3 } & { 3 } & { - 4 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \hat { i } ( 12 - 3 ) - \hat { j } ( - 4 - 3 ) + \hat { k } ( 3 + 9 ){/tex} {tex}= 9 \hat { i } + 7 \hat { j } + 12 \hat { k }{/tex} {tex}| \vec { A B } \times \vec { A C } | = \sqrt { ( 9 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 7 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 12 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex} {tex}= \sqrt { 81 + 49 + 144 }{/tex} {tex} = \sqrt { 274 }{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\Delta A B C = \frac { 1 } { 2 } | \vec { A B } \times \vec { A C } |{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \sqrt { 274 }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { \sqrt { 274 } } { 2 } {/tex} {tex}\therefore \ Area = \frac { \sqrt { 274 } } { 2 } {/tex} sq units.
solution_html:
Given , ΔABC, whose vertices are A (1, 2, 3), B(2, -1, 4) and C(4, 5, -1).

Let the position vectors of the vertices A, B and C of {tex}\Delta A B C{/tex}  is 
{tex}\vec { O A } = \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + \hat { 3 k }{/tex}

{tex} \vec { O B } = 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k }{/tex} and 
{tex}\vec { O C } = 4 \hat { i } + 5 \hat { j } - \hat { k }{/tex}

We know that , {tex}\vec { A B } = \vec { O B } - \vec { O A }{/tex}
{tex}= ( 2 \hat { i } - \hat { j } + 4 \hat { k } ) - ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ){/tex}
{tex}= \hat { i } - 3 \hat { j } + \hat { k }{/tex}
also ,  {tex}\vec { A C } = \vec { O C } - \vec { O A }{/tex}
{tex}= ( 4 \hat { i } + 5 \hat { j } - \hat { k } ) - ( \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k } ){/tex}
{tex}= ( 3 \hat { i } + 3 \hat { j } - 4 \hat { k } ){/tex}
Now, {tex}\vec { A B } \times \vec { A C } = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { \hat { i } } & { \hat { j } } & { \hat { k } } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } & { 1 } \\ { 3 } & { 3 } & { - 4 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
{tex}= \hat { i } ( 12 - 3 ) - \hat { j } ( - 4 - 3 ) + \hat { k } ( 3 + 9 ){/tex}
{tex}= 9 \hat { i } + 7 \hat { j } + 12 \hat { k }{/tex}
{tex}| \vec { A B } \times \vec { A C } | = \sqrt { ( 9 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 7 ) ^ { 2 } + ( 12 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}

{tex}= \sqrt { 81 + 49 + 144 }{/tex}

{tex} = \sqrt { 274 }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of {tex}\Delta A B C = \frac { 1 } { 2 } | \vec { A B } \times \vec { A C } |{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 2 } \sqrt { 274 }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { \sqrt { 274 } } { 2 } {/tex}

{tex}\therefore \ Area = \frac { \sqrt { 274 } } { 2 } {/tex}sq units.

question_html: If {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} are unit vectors, then find the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b },{/tex} given that {tex} ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ){/tex} is a unit vector.
question_text: If {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} are unit vectors, then find the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b },{/tex} given that {tex} ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ){/tex} is a unit vector.
explanation: We have to find the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b },{/tex} given that {tex} ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ){/tex} is a unit vector.Given, {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} are two unit vectors, then {tex} | \vec { a } | = | \vec { b } | = 1{/tex} Also, {tex} ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ){/tex} is a unit vector. {tex} \therefore \quad | \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } | = 1 \Rightarrow | \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 1 ^ { 2 }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ) \cdot ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ) = 1 \quad \left[ \because | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } = \vec { a } \cdot \vec { a } \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3 ( \vec { a } \cdot \vec { a } ) - \sqrt { 3 } ( \vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } ) - \sqrt { 3 } ( \vec { b } \cdot \vec { a } ) + \vec { b } \cdot \vec { b } = 1{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3 | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } - \sqrt { 3 } | \vec { a } | | \vec { b } | \cos \theta{/tex} {tex} - \sqrt 3 |\vec b||\vec a|\cos \theta + |\vec b{|^2} = 1{/tex} where, {tex} \theta{/tex} is the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }.{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3 \times 1 - \sqrt { 3 } \times 1 \times 1 \times \cos \theta - \sqrt { 3 } \times 1 \times 1 \times \cos \theta + 1 = 1{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 3 = 2 \sqrt { 3 } \cos \theta \Rightarrow \cos \theta = \frac { 3 } { 2 \sqrt { 3 } }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos \theta = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } \Rightarrow \theta = \frac { \pi } { 6 }{/tex} Hence, the required angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} is {tex} \frac { \pi } { 6 }.{/tex}
solution: We have to find the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b },{/tex} given that {tex} ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ){/tex} is a unit vector.Given, {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} are two unit vectors, then {tex} | \vec { a } | = | \vec { b } | = 1{/tex} Also, {tex} ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ){/tex} is a unit vector. {tex} \therefore \quad | \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } | = 1 \Rightarrow | \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 1 ^ { 2 }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ) \cdot ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ) = 1 \quad \left[ \because | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } = \vec { a } \cdot \vec { a } \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3 ( \vec { a } \cdot \vec { a } ) - \sqrt { 3 } ( \vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } ) - \sqrt { 3 } ( \vec { b } \cdot \vec { a } ) + \vec { b } \cdot \vec { b } = 1{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3 | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } - \sqrt { 3 } | \vec { a } | | \vec { b } | \cos \theta{/tex} {tex} - \sqrt 3 |\vec b||\vec a|\cos \theta + |\vec b{|^2} = 1{/tex} where, {tex} \theta{/tex} is the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }.{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 3 \times 1 - \sqrt { 3 } \times 1 \times 1 \times \cos \theta - \sqrt { 3 } \times 1 \times 1 \times \cos \theta + 1 = 1{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \quad 3 = 2 \sqrt { 3 } \cos \theta \Rightarrow \cos \theta = \frac { 3 } { 2 \sqrt { 3 } }{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \cos \theta = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } \Rightarrow \theta = \frac { \pi } { 6 }{/tex} Hence, the required angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} is {tex} \frac { \pi } { 6 }.{/tex}
solution_html:
We have to find the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b },{/tex} given that {tex} ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ){/tex} is a unit vector.Given, {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} are two unit vectors, then
{tex} | \vec { a } | = | \vec { b } | = 1{/tex}
Also, {tex} ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ){/tex} is a unit vector.
{tex} \therefore \quad | \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } | = 1 \Rightarrow | \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } | ^ { 2 } = 1 ^ { 2 }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ) \cdot ( \sqrt { 3 } \vec { a } - \vec { b } ) = 1 \quad \left[ \because | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } = \vec { a } \cdot \vec { a } \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 3 ( \vec { a } \cdot \vec { a } ) - \sqrt { 3 } ( \vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } ) - \sqrt { 3 } ( \vec { b } \cdot \vec { a } ) + \vec { b } \cdot \vec { b } = 1{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 3 | \vec { a } | ^ { 2 } - \sqrt { 3 } | \vec { a } | | \vec { b } | \cos \theta{/tex}
{tex} - \sqrt 3 |\vec b||\vec a|\cos \theta + |\vec b{|^2} = 1{/tex}
where, {tex} \theta{/tex} is the angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }.{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 3 \times 1 - \sqrt { 3 } \times 1 \times 1 \times \cos \theta - \sqrt { 3 } \times 1 \times 1 \times \cos \theta + 1 = 1{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad 3 = 2 \sqrt { 3 } \cos \theta \Rightarrow \cos \theta = \frac { 3 } { 2 \sqrt { 3 } }{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \cos \theta = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } \Rightarrow \theta = \frac { \pi } { 6 }{/tex}
Hence, the required angle between {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} and {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} is {tex} \frac { \pi } { 6 }.{/tex}

question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}{/tex} represent two adjacent sides of a parallelogram, find unit vectors parallel to the diagonals of the parallelogram.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}{/tex} represent two adjacent sides of a parallelogram, find unit vectors parallel to the diagonals of the parallelogram.
explanation: Let ABCD be a parallelogram such that {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}=\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{B C}=\vec{b}{/tex} . Then, In ∆ABC, {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}+\overrightarrow{B C}=\overrightarrow{A C}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \overrightarrow{A C}=\vec{a}+\vec{b}=3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} Again, in ∆ABD, {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}+\overrightarrow{B D}=\overrightarrow{A D}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \overrightarrow{B D}=\overrightarrow{A D}-\overrightarrow{A B}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \overrightarrow{B D}=\vec{b}-\vec{a}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}{/tex} Now, {tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad|\overrightarrow{A C}|=\sqrt{9+36+4}=7{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{B D}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad|\overrightarrow{B D}|=\sqrt{1+4+64}=\sqrt{69}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Unit vector along {tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=\frac{\overrightarrow{A C}}{|\overrightarrow{A C}|}=\frac{1}{7}(3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}){/tex} and Unit vector along {tex}\overrightarrow{B D}=\frac{\overrightarrow{B D}}{|\overrightarrow{B D}|}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{69}}(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}){/tex}
solution: Let ABCD be a parallelogram such that {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}=\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{B C}=\vec{b}{/tex} . Then, In ∆ABC, {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}+\overrightarrow{B C}=\overrightarrow{A C}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \overrightarrow{A C}=\vec{a}+\vec{b}=3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} Again, in ∆ABD, {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}+\overrightarrow{B D}=\overrightarrow{A D}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \overrightarrow{B D}=\overrightarrow{A D}-\overrightarrow{A B}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \overrightarrow{B D}=\vec{b}-\vec{a}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}{/tex} Now, {tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad|\overrightarrow{A C}|=\sqrt{9+36+4}=7{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{B D}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad|\overrightarrow{B D}|=\sqrt{1+4+64}=\sqrt{69}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Unit vector along {tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=\frac{\overrightarrow{A C}}{|\overrightarrow{A C}|}=\frac{1}{7}(3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}){/tex} and Unit vector along {tex}\overrightarrow{B D}=\frac{\overrightarrow{B D}}{|\overrightarrow{B D}|}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{69}}(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}){/tex}
solution_html:
Let ABCD be a parallelogram such that {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}=\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{B C}=\vec{b}{/tex}. Then,

In ∆ABC,
{tex}\overrightarrow{A B}+\overrightarrow{B C}=\overrightarrow{A C}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \overrightarrow{A C}=\vec{a}+\vec{b}=3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}
Again, in ∆ABD,

 {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}+\overrightarrow{B D}=\overrightarrow{A D}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \overrightarrow{B D}=\overrightarrow{A D}-\overrightarrow{A B}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \overrightarrow{B D}=\vec{b}-\vec{a}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}{/tex}
Now, {tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad|\overrightarrow{A C}|=\sqrt{9+36+4}=7{/tex}
and {tex}\overrightarrow{B D}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad|\overrightarrow{B D}|=\sqrt{1+4+64}=\sqrt{69}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Unit vector along {tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=\frac{\overrightarrow{A C}}{|\overrightarrow{A C}|}=\frac{1}{7}(3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}){/tex}
and Unit vector along {tex}\overrightarrow{B D}=\frac{\overrightarrow{B D}}{|\overrightarrow{B D}|}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{69}}(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}){/tex}

question_text: ABCD is a rhombus whose diagonals intersect at E. Then {tex}\vec{\mathrm{EA}}+\vec{\mathrm{EB}}+\vec{\mathrm{EC}}+ \vec{\mathrm{ED}}{/tex} equals

options.explanation: ABCD is a rhombus and its diagonal intersects at E. According to the properties of rhombus, {tex}|\vec{\mathrm{AB}}|=|\vec{\mathrm{BC}}|=|\vec{\mathrm{CD}}|=|\vec{\mathrm{DA}}|{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm{EA}}=-\vec{\mathrm{EC}}{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm{ED}}=-\vec{\mathrm{EB}}{/tex} Consider the given expression {tex}\vec{\mathrm{EA}}+\vec{\mathrm{EB}}+\vec{\mathrm{EC}}+\vec{\mathrm{ED}}{/tex} {tex}=-\vec{\mathrm{EC}}+\vec{\mathrm{EB}}+\vec{\mathrm{EC}}-\vec{\mathrm{EB}}{/tex} ......( {tex}\because\vec{\mathrm{EA}}=-\vec{\mathrm{EC}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{ED}}=-\vec{\mathrm{EB}}{/tex} ) = 0 Hence, the correct answer is 0.

explanation: ABCD is a rhombus and its diagonal intersects at E. According to the properties of rhombus, {tex}|\vec{\mathrm{AB}}|=|\vec{\mathrm{BC}}|=|\vec{\mathrm{CD}}|=|\vec{\mathrm{DA}}|{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm{EA}}=-\vec{\mathrm{EC}}{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm{ED}}=-\vec{\mathrm{EB}}{/tex} Consider the given expression {tex}\vec{\mathrm{EA}}+\vec{\mathrm{EB}}+\vec{\mathrm{EC}}+\vec{\mathrm{ED}}{/tex} {tex}=-\vec{\mathrm{EC}}+\vec{\mathrm{EB}}+\vec{\mathrm{EC}}-\vec{\mathrm{EB}}{/tex} ......( {tex}\because\vec{\mathrm{EA}}=-\vec{\mathrm{EC}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{ED}}=-\vec{\mathrm{EB}}{/tex} ) = 0 Hence, the correct answer is 0.

question_text: If {tex}|\vec{a}|=4{/tex} and {tex}-3 \leq \lambda \leq 2{/tex} , then the range of {tex}|\lambda \vec{a}|{/tex} is

options.explanation: Given that, {tex}|\vec{\mathrm{a}}|=4{/tex} and {tex}-3 \leq \lambda \leq 2{/tex} We know that, {tex}|\lambda \vec{\mathrm{a}}|=|\lambda||\vec{\mathrm{a}}|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow|\lambda \vec{a}|=|-3||\vec{a}|=3.4=12{/tex} at {tex}\lambda=-3{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow|\lambda \vec{\mathrm{a}}|=|0||\vec{\mathrm{a}}|=0.4=0{/tex} at {tex} \lambda=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow|\lambda \vec{\mathrm{a}}|=|2||\vec{\mathrm{a}}|=2.4=8{/tex} at {tex} \lambda=2{/tex} Hence, the range of {tex}|\lambda \vec{\mathrm{a}}|{/tex} is [0, 12].

explanation: Given that, {tex}|\vec{\mathrm{a}}|=4{/tex} and {tex}-3 \leq \lambda \leq 2{/tex} We know that, {tex}|\lambda \vec{\mathrm{a}}|=|\lambda||\vec{\mathrm{a}}|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow|\lambda \vec{a}|=|-3||\vec{a}|=3.4=12{/tex} at {tex}\lambda=-3{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow|\lambda \vec{\mathrm{a}}|=|0||\vec{\mathrm{a}}|=0.4=0{/tex} at {tex} \lambda=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow|\lambda \vec{\mathrm{a}}|=|2||\vec{\mathrm{a}}|=2.4=8{/tex} at {tex} \lambda=2{/tex} Hence, the range of {tex}|\lambda \vec{\mathrm{a}}|{/tex} is [0, 12].

question_text: The area of a triangle formed by vertices O, A, and B, where {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{OA}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{OB}}=-3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} is

options.explanation: Area of {tex}\triangle = \left|\frac {\vec {OA} \times \vec {OB}}2\right|{/tex} {tex}{\vec {OA} \times \vec {OB}} = \left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat i & \hat j & \hat k \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \\ -3 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = |i(2 -(-6) - {tex}\hat j{/tex} (1- (-9)) + {tex}\hat k{/tex} (-2 - (-6))| = |8 {tex}\hat i{/tex} - 10 {tex}\hat j{/tex} + 4 {tex}\hat k{/tex} | = {tex}\sqrt {8^2+(-10)^2 + (4)^2}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt {180}{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle = \frac 12|{\vec {OA} \times \vec {OB}}|{/tex} = {tex}\frac 12 \times \sqrt {180}{/tex} = {tex}\frac 12 \times 6\sqrt {5}{/tex} = 3 {tex}\sqrt 5{/tex}

explanation: Area of {tex}\triangle = \left|\frac {\vec {OA} \times \vec {OB}}2\right|{/tex} {tex}{\vec {OA} \times \vec {OB}} = \left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat i & \hat j & \hat k \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \\ -3 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = |i(2 -(-6) - {tex}\hat j{/tex} (1- (-9)) + {tex}\hat k{/tex} (-2 - (-6))| = |8 {tex}\hat i{/tex} - 10 {tex}\hat j{/tex} + 4 {tex}\hat k{/tex} | = {tex}\sqrt {8^2+(-10)^2 + (4)^2}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt {180}{/tex} Area of {tex}\triangle = \frac 12|{\vec {OA} \times \vec {OB}}|{/tex} = {tex}\frac 12 \times \sqrt {180}{/tex} = {tex}\frac 12 \times 6\sqrt {5}{/tex} = 3 {tex}\sqrt 5{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find a vector {tex}\vec r{/tex} equally inclined to the three axes and whose magnitude is {tex}3 \sqrt 3{/tex} units.
question_text: Find a vector {tex}\vec r{/tex} equally inclined to the three axes and whose magnitude is {tex}3 \sqrt 3{/tex} units.
explanation: Since {tex}\overrightarrow r{/tex} is inclined equal angles to axes ∴ {tex}\alpha=\beta=\gamma{/tex} {tex}3\cos^2\alpha=1{/tex} {tex}\cos\alpha=\pm\frac1{\sqrt3}{/tex} Now components in each direction are equal Let {tex}\overrightarrow r=a\widehat i+a\widehat j+a\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{\left|r\right|}=\sqrt{a^2+a^2+a^2}=\sqrt3a{/tex} √3 a = 2√3 a = 2 {tex}\overrightarrow r=2\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k{/tex}
solution: Since {tex}\overrightarrow r{/tex} is inclined equal angles to axes ∴ {tex}\alpha=\beta=\gamma{/tex} {tex}3\cos^2\alpha=1{/tex} {tex}\cos\alpha=\pm\frac1{\sqrt3}{/tex} Now components in each direction are equal Let {tex}\overrightarrow r=a\widehat i+a\widehat j+a\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{\left|r\right|}=\sqrt{a^2+a^2+a^2}=\sqrt3a{/tex} √3 a = 2√3 a = 2 {tex}\overrightarrow r=2\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k{/tex}
solution_html:
Since {tex}\overrightarrow r{/tex} is inclined equal angles to axes 

∴ {tex}\alpha=\beta=\gamma{/tex} 

{tex}3\cos^2\alpha=1{/tex}

{tex}\cos\alpha=\pm\frac1{\sqrt3}{/tex}

Now components in each direction are equal 

Let {tex}\overrightarrow r=a\widehat i+a\widehat j+a\widehat k{/tex} 

{tex}\overrightarrow{\left|r\right|}=\sqrt{a^2+a^2+a^2}=\sqrt3a{/tex}

√3 a = 2√3 

a = 2

{tex}\overrightarrow r=2\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} represent two adjacent sides of a parallelogram, find unit vectors parallel to the diagonals of the parallelogram.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} represent two adjacent sides of a parallelogram, find unit vectors parallel to the diagonals of the parallelogram.
explanation: {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}2\hat{j}{/tex} + {tex}3\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{b}{/tex} = {tex}2\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}4\hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}5\hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{d_1}{/tex} = {tex}\overrightarrow{a_1}{/tex} + {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}2 \hat{j}{/tex} + {tex}3\hat{k}{/tex} + {tex}2\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}4\hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}5\hat{k}{/tex} = {tex}3\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}6\hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}2\hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\hat{d_1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{[3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}]}{\sqrt{3^2+6^2+(-2)^2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}}{7}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{d_2}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{a}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}4 \hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}5 \hat{k}{/tex} - {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} - {tex}2 \hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}3 \hat{k}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}2 \hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}8 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\hat{d}_2{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}]}{\sqrt{1+4+64}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}}{\sqrt{71}}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}2\hat{j}{/tex} + {tex}3\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{b}{/tex} = {tex}2\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}4\hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}5\hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{d_1}{/tex} = {tex}\overrightarrow{a_1}{/tex} + {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}2 \hat{j}{/tex} + {tex}3\hat{k}{/tex} + {tex}2\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}4\hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}5\hat{k}{/tex} = {tex}3\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}6\hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}2\hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\hat{d_1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{[3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}]}{\sqrt{3^2+6^2+(-2)^2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}}{7}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{d_2}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{a}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}4 \hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}5 \hat{k}{/tex} - {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} - {tex}2 \hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}3 \hat{k}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}2 \hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}8 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\hat{d}_2{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}]}{\sqrt{1+4+64}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}}{\sqrt{71}}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\vec{a}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}2\hat{j}{/tex} + {tex}3\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{b}{/tex} = {tex}2\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}4\hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}5\hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{d_1}{/tex} = {tex}\overrightarrow{a_1}{/tex} + {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}{/tex}
= {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}2 \hat{j}{/tex} + {tex}3\hat{k}{/tex} + {tex}2\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}4\hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}5\hat{k}{/tex}
= {tex}3\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}6\hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}2\hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\hat{d_1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{[3 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}]}{\sqrt{3^2+6^2+(-2)^2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\hat{i}+6 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}}{7}{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{d_2}{/tex} = {tex}\vec{b}-\vec{a}{/tex}
= {tex}2 \hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}4 \hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}5 \hat{k}{/tex} - {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} - {tex}2 \hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}3 \hat{k}{/tex}
= {tex}\hat{i}{/tex} + {tex}2 \hat{j}{/tex} - {tex}8 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\hat{d}_2{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}]}{\sqrt{1+4+64}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-8 \hat{k}}{\sqrt{71}}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: The value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} for which the vectors {tex}2 \hat{i}-\lambda \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} are orthogonal is ________.
question_text: The value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} for which the vectors {tex}2 \hat{i}-\lambda \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} are orthogonal is ________.
explanation: For orthogonal vector their dot product must be zero. {tex}(2\hat{i}-\lambda\hat{i}+\hat{k})\cdot(\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = 0 2 - 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} - 1 = 0 -2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 1 = 0 {tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}1 \over 2{/tex}
solution: For orthogonal vector their dot product must be zero. {tex}(2\hat{i}-\lambda\hat{i}+\hat{k})\cdot(\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = 0 2 - 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} - 1 = 0 -2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} + 1 = 0 {tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}1 \over 2{/tex}
solution_html:
For orthogonal vector their dot product must be zero.
{tex}(2\hat{i}-\lambda\hat{i}+\hat{k})\cdot(\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = 0
2 - 2{tex}\lambda{/tex} - 1 = 0
-2{tex}\lambda{/tex} + 1 = 0
{tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}1 \over 2{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find {tex}|\vec{a}| \text { and }|\vec{b}|, \text { if }{/tex} {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{a}-\vec{b})=12 \text { and }|\vec{a}|=2|\vec{b}|{/tex}
question_text: Find {tex}|\vec{a}| \text { and }|\vec{b}|, \text { if }{/tex} {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{a}-\vec{b})=12 \text { and }|\vec{a}|=2|\vec{b}|{/tex}
explanation: Given, {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{a}-\vec{b})=12 \text { and }|\vec{a}|=2|\vec{b}|{/tex} ..........(1) Now, {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{a}-\vec{b})=12{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}|^{2}-|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex} {tex}(2|\vec{b}|)^{2}-|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex} . [From (1)] {tex}4|\vec{b}|^{2}-|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex} {tex}3|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex} {tex}|\vec{b}|^{2}=\frac{12}{3}{/tex} {tex}|\vec{b}|^{2}=4{/tex} {tex}|\vec{b}|=2{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}|=2|\vec{b}|=2(2){/tex} {tex}\therefore |\vec{a}|=4 ~\&~ |\vec{b}|=2{/tex}
solution: Given, {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{a}-\vec{b})=12 \text { and }|\vec{a}|=2|\vec{b}|{/tex} ..........(1) Now, {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{a}-\vec{b})=12{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}|^{2}-|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex} {tex}(2|\vec{b}|)^{2}-|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex} . [From (1)] {tex}4|\vec{b}|^{2}-|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex} {tex}3|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex} {tex}|\vec{b}|^{2}=\frac{12}{3}{/tex} {tex}|\vec{b}|^{2}=4{/tex} {tex}|\vec{b}|=2{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}|=2|\vec{b}|=2(2){/tex} {tex}\therefore |\vec{a}|=4 ~\&~ |\vec{b}|=2{/tex}
solution_html:
Given,
{tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{a}-\vec{b})=12 \text { and }|\vec{a}|=2|\vec{b}|{/tex}..........(1)
Now,
 {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{a}-\vec{b})=12{/tex}
{tex}|\vec{a}|^{2}-|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex}
{tex}(2|\vec{b}|)^{2}-|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex}.  [From (1)]
{tex}4|\vec{b}|^{2}-|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex}
{tex}3|\vec{b}|^{2}=12{/tex}
{tex}|\vec{b}|^{2}=\frac{12}{3}{/tex}
{tex}|\vec{b}|^{2}=4{/tex}
{tex}|\vec{b}|=2{/tex}
{tex}|\vec{a}|=2|\vec{b}|=2(2){/tex}
{tex}\therefore |\vec{a}|=4 ~\&~ |\vec{b}|=2{/tex}
