question_html: Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} for which the vectors {tex}(2 \hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}(3 \hat i+2 \hat j-4 \hat k){/tex} are perpendicular to each other.
question_text: Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} for which the vectors {tex}(2 \hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}(3 \hat i+2 \hat j-4 \hat k){/tex} are perpendicular to each other.
explanation: We have, {tex}(2 \hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}){/tex} {tex}(3 \hat i+2 \hat j-4 \hat k){/tex} since these two vectors are perpendicular, their dot product will be zero. {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \theta{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \frac{\pi}{2}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{\imath}+\lambda \hat{\jmath}+3 \hat{k}) \cdot(3 \hat{\imath}+2 \hat{\jmath}-4 \hat{k}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} =(2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3)+( {tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2)+(3 {tex}\times{/tex} -4) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 6 + 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} -12 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6 = 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}\frac{6}{2}=3{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3
solution: We have, {tex}(2 \hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}){/tex} {tex}(3 \hat i+2 \hat j-4 \hat k){/tex} since these two vectors are perpendicular, their dot product will be zero. {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \theta{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \frac{\pi}{2}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{\imath}+\lambda \hat{\jmath}+3 \hat{k}) \cdot(3 \hat{\imath}+2 \hat{\jmath}-4 \hat{k}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} =(2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3)+( {tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2)+(3 {tex}\times{/tex} -4) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 6 + 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} -12 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6 = 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}\frac{6}{2}=3{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3
solution_html:
We have,{tex}(2 \hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}(3 \hat i+2 \hat j-4 \hat k){/tex}
since these two vectors are perpendicular, their dot product will be zero.
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \theta{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \frac{\pi}{2}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{\imath}+\lambda \hat{\jmath}+3 \hat{k}) \cdot(3 \hat{\imath}+2 \hat{\jmath}-4 \hat{k}){/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} =(2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3)+({tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2)+(3 {tex}\times{/tex} -4) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 6 + 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex} -12 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6 = 2 {tex}\lambda{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = {tex}\frac{6}{2}=3{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the area of the triangle whose vertices are A (3, -1, 2), B ( 1, - 1, - 3 ) and C(4, -3, 1).
question_text: Find the area of the triangle whose vertices are A (3, -1, 2), B ( 1, - 1, - 3 ) and C(4, -3, 1).
explanation: Let {tex}\vec{a}, \vec{b} {/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} be the position vectors of vertices A, B and C of ∆ABC respectively. Then, {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}, \vec{b}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=4 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} We know that: Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex} ABC {tex}=\frac{1}{2}|\overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}|{/tex} Now, {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}=\vec{b}-\vec{a}=(\hat{i}-\hat{j}-3 \hat{k})-(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} {tex}=-2 \hat{i}+0 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=\vec{c}-\vec{a}=(4 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+\hat{k})-(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}-2\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}=\left|\begin{array}{rrr} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ -2 & 0 & -5 \\ 1 & -2 & -1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}=(0-10) \hat{i}-(2+5) \hat{j}+(4-0) \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}=-10 \hat{i}-7 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow |\overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}|=\sqrt{(-10)^{2}+(-7)^{2}+4^{2}}=\sqrt{165}{/tex} Hence, area of {tex}\triangle{/tex} ABC {tex}=\frac{1}{2}|\overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}|=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{165}{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}\vec{a}, \vec{b} {/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} be the position vectors of vertices A, B and C of ∆ABC respectively. Then, {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}, \vec{b}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=4 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} We know that: Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex} ABC {tex}=\frac{1}{2}|\overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}|{/tex} Now, {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}=\vec{b}-\vec{a}=(\hat{i}-\hat{j}-3 \hat{k})-(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} {tex}=-2 \hat{i}+0 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=\vec{c}-\vec{a}=(4 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+\hat{k})-(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} = {tex}\hat{i}-2\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}=\left|\begin{array}{rrr} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ -2 & 0 & -5 \\ 1 & -2 & -1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}=(0-10) \hat{i}-(2+5) \hat{j}+(4-0) \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}=-10 \hat{i}-7 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow |\overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}|=\sqrt{(-10)^{2}+(-7)^{2}+4^{2}}=\sqrt{165}{/tex} Hence, area of {tex}\triangle{/tex} ABC {tex}=\frac{1}{2}|\overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}|=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{165}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\vec{a}, \vec{b} {/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}{/tex} be the position vectors of vertices A, B and C of ∆ABC respectively. Then,
{tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}, \vec{b}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}-3 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=4 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}
We know that:
Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC {tex}=\frac{1}{2}|\overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}|{/tex}
Now,
{tex}\overrightarrow{A B}=\vec{b}-\vec{a}=(\hat{i}-\hat{j}-3 \hat{k})-(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex}{tex}=-2 \hat{i}+0 \hat{j}-5 \hat{k}{/tex}
and  {tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=\vec{c}-\vec{a}=(4 \hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+\hat{k})-(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex}= {tex}\hat{i}-2\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}=\left|\begin{array}{rrr} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ -2 & 0 & -5 \\ 1 & -2 & -1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}=(0-10) \hat{i}-(2+5) \hat{j}+(4-0) \hat{k}{/tex}{tex}=-10 \hat{i}-7 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow |\overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}|=\sqrt{(-10)^{2}+(-7)^{2}+4^{2}}=\sqrt{165}{/tex}
Hence, area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC {tex}=\frac{1}{2}|\overrightarrow{A B} \times \overrightarrow{A C}|=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{165}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}=(\hat{i}-\hat{j}), \vec{b}=(3\hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=(7 \hat{i}-\hat{k}){/tex}, find a vector {tex}\vec{d}{/tex} which is perpendicular to both {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} and for which {tex}\vec{c} \cdot \vec{d}{/tex} = 1.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}=(\hat{i}-\hat{j}), \vec{b}=(3\hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=(7 \hat{i}-\hat{k}){/tex} , find a vector {tex}\vec{d}{/tex} which is perpendicular to both {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} and for which {tex}\vec{c} \cdot \vec{d}{/tex} = 1.
explanation: Let {tex}\vec{a}=a_{1} \hat{i}+a_{2} \hat{j}+a_{3} \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec{d} \perp \vec{a}, \vec{d} \cdot \vec{a}=0 \quad \Rightarrow\left(a_{1} \hat{\imath}+a_{2} \hat{\jmath}+a_{3} \hat{x}\right)(\hat{i}-\hat{j})=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a 1 - a 2 = 0 ...(i) {tex}\vec{d} \perp \vec{b}, \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=0 \quad \Rightarrow\left(a_{1} \hat{i}+a_{2} \hat{j}+a_{3} \hat{k}\right)(3 j-\hat{k})=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3a 2 - a 2 = 0 ...(ii) {tex}\vec{d} \vec{c}=1 \quad \Rightarrow\left(a_{1} \hat{i}+a_{2} \hat{j}+a_{3} \hat{k}\right)(7 \hat{i}-\hat{k})=1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7a 1 - a 3 = 1 ...(iii) Solving equation (i) and (ii) we get 3a 1 - a 3 = 0 ...(iv) Again solving equation (iii) & (iv) we get {tex}a_{1}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex} From equation (i), a 1 - a 2 = 0 or a 1 = a 2 = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} From equation (ii), 3a 2 - a 2 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow 3 \cdot \frac{1}{4}=a_{3} \Rightarrow a_{3}=\frac{3}{4}{/tex} Hence, {tex}\vec{d}=\frac{1}{4} \hat{i}+\frac{1}{4} \hat{j}+\frac{3}{4} \hat{k}{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}\vec{a}=a_{1} \hat{i}+a_{2} \hat{j}+a_{3} \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec{d} \perp \vec{a}, \vec{d} \cdot \vec{a}=0 \quad \Rightarrow\left(a_{1} \hat{\imath}+a_{2} \hat{\jmath}+a_{3} \hat{x}\right)(\hat{i}-\hat{j})=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a 1 - a 2 = 0 ...(i) {tex}\vec{d} \perp \vec{b}, \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=0 \quad \Rightarrow\left(a_{1} \hat{i}+a_{2} \hat{j}+a_{3} \hat{k}\right)(3 j-\hat{k})=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3a 2 - a 2 = 0 ...(ii) {tex}\vec{d} \vec{c}=1 \quad \Rightarrow\left(a_{1} \hat{i}+a_{2} \hat{j}+a_{3} \hat{k}\right)(7 \hat{i}-\hat{k})=1{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7a 1 - a 3 = 1 ...(iii) Solving equation (i) and (ii) we get 3a 1 - a 3 = 0 ...(iv) Again solving equation (iii) & (iv) we get {tex}a_{1}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex} From equation (i), a 1 - a 2 = 0 or a 1 = a 2 = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} From equation (ii), 3a 2 - a 2 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow 3 \cdot \frac{1}{4}=a_{3} \Rightarrow a_{3}=\frac{3}{4}{/tex} Hence, {tex}\vec{d}=\frac{1}{4} \hat{i}+\frac{1}{4} \hat{j}+\frac{3}{4} \hat{k}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\vec{a}=a_{1} \hat{i}+a_{2} \hat{j}+a_{3} \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{d} \perp \vec{a}, \vec{d} \cdot \vec{a}=0 \quad \Rightarrow\left(a_{1} \hat{\imath}+a_{2} \hat{\jmath}+a_{3} \hat{x}\right)(\hat{i}-\hat{j})=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}a1 - a2 = 0 ...(i)
{tex}\vec{d} \perp \vec{b}, \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=0 \quad \Rightarrow\left(a_{1} \hat{i}+a_{2} \hat{j}+a_{3} \hat{k}\right)(3 j-\hat{k})=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3a2 - a2 = 0 ...(ii)
{tex}\vec{d} \vec{c}=1 \quad \Rightarrow\left(a_{1} \hat{i}+a_{2} \hat{j}+a_{3} \hat{k}\right)(7 \hat{i}-\hat{k})=1{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 7a1 - a3 = 1 ...(iii)
Solving equation (i) and (ii) we get 3a1 - a3 = 0 ...(iv)
Again solving equation (iii) & (iv) we get {tex}a_{1}=\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
From equation (i), a1 - a2 = 0 or a1 = a2 = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex}
From equation (ii), 3a2 - a2 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow 3 \cdot \frac{1}{4}=a_{3} \Rightarrow a_{3}=\frac{3}{4}{/tex}
Hence, {tex}\vec{d}=\frac{1}{4} \hat{i}+\frac{1}{4} \hat{j}+\frac{3}{4} \hat{k}{/tex}

solution: Given, {tex}\vec { a } = \hat { - i } + \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = 2 \hat { i } + 3 \hat { j } - \hat { k }{/tex} We know that dot product is given by {tex}\vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } = ( - \hat { i } + \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k } ) \cdot ( 2 \hat { i } + 3 \hat { j } - \hat { k } ){/tex} =-2+ 3+ 2= 3
solution_html:
Given, {tex}\vec { a } = \hat { - i } + \hat { j } - 2 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = 2 \hat { i } + 3 \hat { j } - \hat { k }{/tex}

We know that dot product is given by

question_html: Find the projection of {tex}\vec { a } \text { on } \vec { b },{/tex} if {tex}\vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } = 8 {/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = 2 \hat { i } + 6 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex}
question_text: Find the projection of {tex}\vec { a } \text { on } \vec { b },{/tex} if {tex}\vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } = 8 {/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = 2 \hat { i } + 6 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }.{/tex}
explanation: We are given that, {tex}\vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } = 8{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = 2 \hat { i } + 6 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} The projection of {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} on {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} is given as = {tex}\frac { \vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } } { | \vec { b } | }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 8 } { \sqrt { 2 ^ { 2 } + 6 ^ { 2 } + 3 ^ { 2 } } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 8 } { \sqrt { 4 + 36 + 9 } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 8 } { \sqrt { 49 } } = \frac { 8 } { 7 }{/tex}
solution: We are given that, {tex}\vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } = 8{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = 2 \hat { i } + 6 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} The projection of {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} on {tex} \vec { b }{/tex} is given as = {tex}\frac { \vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } } { | \vec { b } | }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 8 } { \sqrt { 2 ^ { 2 } + 6 ^ { 2 } + 3 ^ { 2 } } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 8 } { \sqrt { 4 + 36 + 9 } }{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 8 } { \sqrt { 49 } } = \frac { 8 } { 7 }{/tex}
solution_html:
We are given that, {tex}\vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } = 8{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = 2 \hat { i } + 6 \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The projection of {tex} \vec { a }{/tex} on {tex} \vec { b }{/tex}  is given as = {tex}\frac { \vec { a } \cdot \vec { b } } { | \vec { b } | }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 8 } { \sqrt { 2 ^ { 2 } + 6 ^ { 2 } + 3 ^ { 2 } } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 8 } { \sqrt { 4 + 36 + 9 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 8 } { \sqrt { 49 } } = \frac { 8 } { 7 }{/tex}

question_html: Find the value of p, if {tex}( 2 \hat { i } + 6 \hat { j } + 27 \hat { k } ) \times ( \hat { i } + 3 \hat { j } + p \hat { k } ) = \vec { 0 }.{/tex}
question_text: Find the value of p, if {tex}( 2 \hat { i } + 6 \hat { j } + 27 \hat { k } ) \times ( \hat { i } + 3 \hat { j } + p \hat { k } ) = \vec { 0 }.{/tex}
explanation: Vector (2 {tex}\hat i{/tex} + 6 {tex}\hat j{/tex} + 27 {tex}\hat k{/tex} ) {tex}\times{/tex} ( {tex}\hat i{/tex} + 3 {tex}\hat j{/tex} + p {tex}\hat k{/tex} ) = {tex}\vec 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{\hat{i}} & {\hat{j}} & {\hat{k}} \\ {2} & {6} & {27} \\ {1} & {3} & {p}\end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\vec 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (6p - 81) {tex}\hat i{/tex} - (2p - 27) {tex}\hat j{/tex} + 0 {tex}\hat k{/tex} = {tex}\vec 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6p - 81 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = {tex}\frac{81}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{27}{2}{/tex}
solution: Vector (2 {tex}\hat i{/tex} + 6 {tex}\hat j{/tex} + 27 {tex}\hat k{/tex} ) {tex}\times{/tex} ( {tex}\hat i{/tex} + 3 {tex}\hat j{/tex} + p {tex}\hat k{/tex} ) = {tex}\vec 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{\hat{i}} & {\hat{j}} & {\hat{k}} \\ {2} & {6} & {27} \\ {1} & {3} & {p}\end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\vec 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (6p - 81) {tex}\hat i{/tex} - (2p - 27) {tex}\hat j{/tex} + 0 {tex}\hat k{/tex} = {tex}\vec 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6p - 81 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = {tex}\frac{81}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{27}{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Vector (2{tex}\hat i{/tex} + 6{tex}\hat j{/tex} + 27{tex}\hat k{/tex}) {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\hat i{/tex} + 3{tex}\hat j{/tex} + p{tex}\hat k{/tex}) = {tex}\vec 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{\hat{i}} & {\hat{j}} & {\hat{k}} \\ {2} & {6} & {27} \\ {1} & {3} & {p}\end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\vec 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (6p - 81){tex}\hat i{/tex} - (2p - 27){tex}\hat j{/tex} + 0{tex}\hat k{/tex} = {tex}\vec 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6p - 81 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = {tex}\frac{81}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{27}{2}{/tex}

question_html: Write the value of p, for which {tex}\vec { a } = 3 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 9 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = \hat { i } + p \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }{/tex} are parallel vectors.
question_text: Write the value of p, for which {tex}\vec { a } = 3 \hat { i } + 2 \hat { j } + 9 \hat { k }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b } = \hat { i } + p \hat { j } + 3 \hat { k }{/tex} are parallel vectors.
explanation: Since vector {tex}\vec a{/tex} {tex}\parallel{/tex} {tex}\vec b{/tex} therefore vector {tex}\vec a{/tex} = {tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\vec b{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 {tex}\hat i{/tex} + 2 {tex}\hat j{/tex} + 9 {tex}\hat k{/tex} = {tex}\lambda{/tex} ( {tex}\hat i{/tex} + p {tex}\hat j{/tex} + 3 {tex}\hat k{/tex} ) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3, 2 = {tex}\lambda{/tex} p, 9 = 3 {tex}\lambda{/tex} or {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3, p = {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
solution: Since vector {tex}\vec a{/tex} {tex}\parallel{/tex} {tex}\vec b{/tex} therefore vector {tex}\vec a{/tex} = {tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\vec b{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 {tex}\hat i{/tex} + 2 {tex}\hat j{/tex} + 9 {tex}\hat k{/tex} = {tex}\lambda{/tex} ( {tex}\hat i{/tex} + p {tex}\hat j{/tex} + 3 {tex}\hat k{/tex} ) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3, 2 = {tex}\lambda{/tex} p, 9 = 3 {tex}\lambda{/tex} or {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3, p = {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
solution_html:
Since vector {tex}\vec a{/tex} {tex}\parallel{/tex} {tex}\vec b{/tex} therefore vector {tex}\vec a{/tex} = {tex}\lambda{/tex} {tex}\vec b{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3{tex}\hat i{/tex} + 2{tex}\hat j{/tex} + 9{tex}\hat k{/tex} = {tex}\lambda{/tex}({tex}\hat i{/tex} + p{tex}\hat j{/tex} + 3{tex}\hat k{/tex})
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3, 2 = {tex}\lambda{/tex}p, 9 = 3{tex}\lambda{/tex}
or {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3, p = {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}

question_html: Find the angle between{tex}\vec { a }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b }{/tex} with magnitudes 1 and 2 respectively, when {tex}| \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | = \sqrt { 3 }.{/tex}
question_text: Find the angle between {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b }{/tex} with magnitudes 1 and 2 respectively, when {tex}| \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | = \sqrt { 3 }.{/tex}
explanation: Here we are given that, {tex}| \vec { a } | = 1 , \vec { | b } | = 2 \text { and } | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | = \sqrt { 3 }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow | \vec { a } | | \vec { b } | \sin \theta = \sqrt { 3 }{/tex} {tex}[ \because \vec { a } \times \vec { b } = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \sin \theta \hat { n } \text { and } | \hat { n } | = 1 ]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 1 \times 2 \times \sin \theta = \sqrt { 3 }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \sin \theta = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } = \sin \frac { \pi } { 3 } \Rightarrow \theta = \frac { \pi } { 3 }{/tex} Hence, the angle between {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b }{/tex} is {tex}\frac { \pi } { 3 }.{/tex}
solution: Here we are given that, {tex}| \vec { a } | = 1 , \vec { | b } | = 2 \text { and } | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | = \sqrt { 3 }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow | \vec { a } | | \vec { b } | \sin \theta = \sqrt { 3 }{/tex} {tex}[ \because \vec { a } \times \vec { b } = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \sin \theta \hat { n } \text { and } | \hat { n } | = 1 ]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 1 \times 2 \times \sin \theta = \sqrt { 3 }{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \sin \theta = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } = \sin \frac { \pi } { 3 } \Rightarrow \theta = \frac { \pi } { 3 }{/tex} Hence, the angle between {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b }{/tex} is {tex}\frac { \pi } { 3 }.{/tex}
solution_html:
Here we are given that, {tex}| \vec { a } | = 1 , \vec { | b } | = 2 \text { and } | \vec { a } \times \vec { b } | = \sqrt { 3 }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow | \vec { a } | | \vec { b } | \sin \theta = \sqrt { 3 }{/tex}
{tex}[ \because \vec { a } \times \vec { b } = | \vec { a } \| \vec { b } | \sin \theta \hat { n } \text { and } | \hat { n } | = 1 ]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 1 \times 2 \times \sin \theta = \sqrt { 3 }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \sin \theta = \frac { \sqrt { 3 } } { 2 } = \sin \frac { \pi } { 3 } \Rightarrow \theta = \frac { \pi } { 3 }{/tex}
Hence, the angle between {tex}\vec { a }{/tex} and {tex}\vec { b }{/tex} is {tex}\frac { \pi } { 3 }.{/tex}

question_html: Write the value of p for which {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+9 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\mathrm{pj}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} are parallel vectors.
question_text: Write the value of p for which {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+9 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\mathrm{pj}+3 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} are parallel vectors.
explanation: {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{\imath}+2 \hat{\jmath}+9 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{\imath}+p \hat{\jmath}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} for {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} to be parallel to {tex}\vec{b} {/tex} i.e. {tex}(\vec{a} ×\vec{b})=0{/tex} [vector cross product] {tex}\begin{array}{lll} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 3 & 2 & 9 \\ 1 & p & 3 \end{array}{/tex} = 0 {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}(6-9 p)-\hat{\mathrm{j}}(9-9)+\hat{k}(3 p-2){/tex} =0 {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}(6-9 p)+\hat{k}(3 p-2){/tex} = 0 {tex}\hat{\imath}+0 \hat{k}{/tex} (6 - 9p) = 0 and (3p - 2) = 0 Therefore, {tex}p=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{\imath}+2 \hat{\jmath}+9 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{\imath}+p \hat{\jmath}+3 \hat{k}{/tex} for {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} to be parallel to {tex}\vec{b} {/tex} i.e. {tex}(\vec{a} ×\vec{b})=0{/tex} [vector cross product] {tex}\begin{array}{lll} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 3 & 2 & 9 \\ 1 & p & 3 \end{array}{/tex} = 0 {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}(6-9 p)-\hat{\mathrm{j}}(9-9)+\hat{k}(3 p-2){/tex} =0 {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}(6-9 p)+\hat{k}(3 p-2){/tex} = 0 {tex}\hat{\imath}+0 \hat{k}{/tex} (6 - 9p) = 0 and (3p - 2) = 0 Therefore, {tex}p=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{\imath}+2 \hat{\jmath}+9 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{\imath}+p \hat{\jmath}+3 \hat{k}{/tex}
for {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} to be parallel to {tex}\vec{b} {/tex}
i.e. {tex}(\vec{a} ×\vec{b})=0{/tex} [vector cross product]
{tex}\begin{array}{lll} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 3 & 2 & 9 \\ 1 & p & 3 \end{array}{/tex} = 0
{tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}(6-9 p)-\hat{\mathrm{j}}(9-9)+\hat{k}(3 p-2){/tex} =0
{tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}(6-9 p)+\hat{k}(3 p-2){/tex} = 0{tex}\hat{\imath}+0 \hat{k}{/tex}
(6 - 9p) = 0 and (3p - 2) = 0
Therefore, {tex}p=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}

question_html: Write a unit vector in the direction of {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}{/tex}.
question_text: Write a unit vector in the direction of {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}{/tex} .
explanation: Given vector is , {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}|=\sqrt{3^{2}+(-2)^{2}+6^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{9+4+36}=\sqrt{49}=7{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Unit vector in the direction of {tex}\vec{a}=\widehat{a}=\frac{\vec{a}}{|\vec{a}|}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{7}(3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex} {tex}=\frac{3}{7} \hat{i}-\frac{2}{7} \hat{j}+\frac{6}{7} \hat{k}{/tex}
solution: Given vector is , {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}|\vec{a}|=\sqrt{3^{2}+(-2)^{2}+6^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{9+4+36}=\sqrt{49}=7{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Unit vector in the direction of {tex}\vec{a}=\widehat{a}=\frac{\vec{a}}{|\vec{a}|}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{7}(3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+6 \hat{k}){/tex} {tex}=\frac{3}{7} \hat{i}-\frac{2}{7} \hat{j}+\frac{6}{7} \hat{k}{/tex}

question_html: Find {tex}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|{/tex}, if {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}-7 \hat{j}+7 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}.
question_text: Find {tex}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|{/tex} , if {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}-7 \hat{j}+7 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} .
explanation: Given that {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{1}-7 \hat{\jmath}+7 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}=3 \hat{1}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {\hat{1}} & {\hat{\jmath}} & {\hat{k}} \\ {1} & {-7} & {7} \\ {3} & {-2} & {2} \end{array}\right|{/tex} Expanding along first row, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}[(-7 \times 2)-(-2 \times 7)]-\hat{\mathrm{j}}[(1 \times 2)-(7 \times 3)]+\hat{\mathrm{k}}[(-2 \times 1)-(-7 \times 3)]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=(0) \hat{\mathrm{i}}-(-19) \hat{\mathrm{j}}+(19) \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=0 \hat{i}+19 \hat{\jmath}+19 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore|\vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \vec{\mathrm{b}}|=\sqrt{0^{2}+19^{2}+19^{2}}=19 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
solution: Given that {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{1}-7 \hat{\jmath}+7 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}=3 \hat{1}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {\hat{1}} & {\hat{\jmath}} & {\hat{k}} \\ {1} & {-7} & {7} \\ {3} & {-2} & {2} \end{array}\right|{/tex} Expanding along first row, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}[(-7 \times 2)-(-2 \times 7)]-\hat{\mathrm{j}}[(1 \times 2)-(7 \times 3)]+\hat{\mathrm{k}}[(-2 \times 1)-(-7 \times 3)]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=(0) \hat{\mathrm{i}}-(-19) \hat{\mathrm{j}}+(19) \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=0 \hat{i}+19 \hat{\jmath}+19 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} {tex}\therefore|\vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \vec{\mathrm{b}}|=\sqrt{0^{2}+19^{2}+19^{2}}=19 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given that {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{1}-7 \hat{\jmath}+7 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}=3 \hat{1}-2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} 
{tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {\hat{1}} & {\hat{\jmath}} & {\hat{k}} \\ {1} & {-7} & {7} \\ {3} & {-2} & {2} \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
Expanding along first row,
{tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{\mathrm{i}}[(-7 \times 2)-(-2 \times 7)]-\hat{\mathrm{j}}[(1 \times 2)-(7 \times 3)]+\hat{\mathrm{k}}[(-2 \times 1)-(-7 \times 3)]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=(0) \hat{\mathrm{i}}-(-19) \hat{\mathrm{j}}+(19) \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=0 \hat{i}+19 \hat{\jmath}+19 \hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore|\vec{\mathrm{a}} \times \vec{\mathrm{b}}|=\sqrt{0^{2}+19^{2}+19^{2}}=19 \sqrt{2}{/tex}

question_html: Show that the area of a parallelogram having diagonals {tex}3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex} is {tex}5 \sqrt{3}{/tex}  square untis.
question_text: Show that the area of a parallelogram having diagonals {tex}3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex} is {tex}5 \sqrt{3}{/tex} square untis.
explanation: Let {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex} . Then, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\left|\begin{array}{rrr} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 3 & 1 & -2 \\ 1 & -3 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}=(4-6) \hat{i}-(12+2) \hat{j}+(-9-1) \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}=-2 \hat{i}-14 \hat{j}-10 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow |\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=\sqrt{(-2)^{2}+(-14)^{2}+(-10)^{2}}=\sqrt{300}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of the parallelogram {tex}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{300}{/tex} {tex}=5 \sqrt{3}{/tex} sq. units.
solution: Let {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex} . Then, {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\left|\begin{array}{rrr} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 3 & 1 & -2 \\ 1 & -3 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}=(4-6) \hat{i}-(12+2) \hat{j}+(-9-1) \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}=-2 \hat{i}-14 \hat{j}-10 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow |\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=\sqrt{(-2)^{2}+(-14)^{2}+(-10)^{2}}=\sqrt{300}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of the parallelogram {tex}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{300}{/tex} {tex}=5 \sqrt{3}{/tex} sq. units.

Let {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}-3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}{/tex}. Then,
{tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\left|\begin{array}{rrr} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 3 & 1 & -2 \\ 1 & -3 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex}{tex}=(4-6) \hat{i}-(12+2) \hat{j}+(-9-1) \hat{k}{/tex}{tex}=-2 \hat{i}-14 \hat{j}-10 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow |\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=\sqrt{(-2)^{2}+(-14)^{2}+(-10)^{2}}=\sqrt{300}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Area of the parallelogram {tex}=\frac{1}{2}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{300}{/tex} {tex}=5 \sqrt{3}{/tex} sq. units.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find the angle between the vectors {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}
question_text: Find the angle between the vectors {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}
explanation: By vector dot product, we know that: {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=|a||b| \cos \theta{/tex} {tex}\cos \theta=\frac{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}}{|a||b|}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=-1{/tex} {tex}|a|=\sqrt{3}{/tex} {tex}|b|=\sqrt{3}{/tex} Thus, {tex}\cos \theta=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{3} .\sqrt{3}}{/tex} {tex}\cos \theta=\frac{-1}{3}{/tex} So, {tex}\theta=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{-1}{3}\right){/tex}
solution: By vector dot product, we know that: {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=|a||b| \cos \theta{/tex} {tex}\cos \theta=\frac{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}}{|a||b|}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=-1{/tex} {tex}|a|=\sqrt{3}{/tex} {tex}|b|=\sqrt{3}{/tex} Thus, {tex}\cos \theta=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{3} .\sqrt{3}}{/tex} {tex}\cos \theta=\frac{-1}{3}{/tex} So, {tex}\theta=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{-1}{3}\right){/tex}
solution_html:
By vector dot product, we know that:
{tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=|a||b| \cos \theta{/tex}
{tex}\cos \theta=\frac{\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}}{|a||b|}{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=-1{/tex}
{tex}|a|=\sqrt{3}{/tex}{tex}|b|=\sqrt{3}{/tex}
Thus,
{tex}\cos \theta=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{3} .\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\cos \theta=\frac{-1}{3}{/tex}
So, {tex}\theta=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{-1}{3}\right){/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0}{/tex}, prove that {tex}(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})=(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})=(\vec{c} \times \vec{a}){/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0}{/tex} , prove that {tex}(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})=(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})=(\vec{c} \times \vec{a}){/tex} .
explanation: Given, {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0} \Rightarrow \vec{a}+\vec{b}=-\vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \times \vec{b}=(-\vec{c}) \times \vec{b}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})+(\vec{b} \times \vec{b})=(-\vec{c}) \times \vec{b}{/tex} [by the distributive law] {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})+\overrightarrow{0}=(\vec{b} \times \vec{c}){/tex} [ {tex}\because \quad \vec{b} \times \vec{b}=\overrightarrow{0}{/tex} and {tex}(-\vec{c}) \times \vec{b}=\vec{b} \times \vec{c}{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{b} \times \vec{c}{/tex} ...(i) Also, {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0} \Rightarrow \vec{b}+\vec{c}=-\vec{a}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{b}+\vec{c}) \times \vec{c}=(-\vec{a}) \times \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})+(\vec{c} \times \vec{c})=(-\vec{a}) \times \vec{c}{/tex} [by the distributive law] {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})+\overrightarrow{0}=\vec{c} \times\vec{a}{/tex} [ {tex}\because\vec{c} \times \vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0}{/tex} and {tex}(-\vec{a}) \times \vec{c}=\vec{c} \times \vec{a}{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{b} \times \vec{c}=\vec{c} \times\vec{a}{/tex} ...(ii) From (i) and (ii), we get {tex}\vec{a} \times\vec{b}=\vec{b} \times\vec{c}=\vec{c} \times\vec {a}{/tex}
solution: Given, {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0} \Rightarrow \vec{a}+\vec{b}=-\vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \times \vec{b}=(-\vec{c}) \times \vec{b}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})+(\vec{b} \times \vec{b})=(-\vec{c}) \times \vec{b}{/tex} [by the distributive law] {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})+\overrightarrow{0}=(\vec{b} \times \vec{c}){/tex} [ {tex}\because \quad \vec{b} \times \vec{b}=\overrightarrow{0}{/tex} and {tex}(-\vec{c}) \times \vec{b}=\vec{b} \times \vec{c}{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{b} \times \vec{c}{/tex} ...(i) Also, {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0} \Rightarrow \vec{b}+\vec{c}=-\vec{a}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{b}+\vec{c}) \times \vec{c}=(-\vec{a}) \times \vec{c}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})+(\vec{c} \times \vec{c})=(-\vec{a}) \times \vec{c}{/tex} [by the distributive law] {tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})+\overrightarrow{0}=\vec{c} \times\vec{a}{/tex} [ {tex}\because\vec{c} \times \vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0}{/tex} and {tex}(-\vec{a}) \times \vec{c}=\vec{c} \times \vec{a}{/tex} ] {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{b} \times \vec{c}=\vec{c} \times\vec{a}{/tex} ...(ii) From (i) and (ii), we get {tex}\vec{a} \times\vec{b}=\vec{b} \times\vec{c}=\vec{c} \times\vec {a}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given, 

{tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0} \Rightarrow \vec{a}+\vec{b}=-\vec{c}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \times \vec{b}=(-\vec{c}) \times \vec{b}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})+(\vec{b} \times \vec{b})=(-\vec{c}) \times \vec{b}{/tex} [by the distributive law]
{tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})+\overrightarrow{0}=(\vec{b} \times \vec{c}){/tex} [{tex}\because \quad \vec{b} \times \vec{b}=\overrightarrow{0}{/tex} and {tex}(-\vec{c}) \times \vec{b}=\vec{b} \times \vec{c}{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{b} \times \vec{c}{/tex} ...(i)
Also, {tex}\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0} \Rightarrow \vec{b}+\vec{c}=-\vec{a}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{b}+\vec{c}) \times \vec{c}=(-\vec{a}) \times \vec{c}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})+(\vec{c} \times \vec{c})=(-\vec{a}) \times \vec{c}{/tex} [by the distributive law]
{tex}\Rightarrow(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})+\overrightarrow{0}=\vec{c} \times\vec{a}{/tex} [{tex}\because\vec{c} \times \vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0}{/tex} and {tex}(-\vec{a}) \times \vec{c}=\vec{c} \times \vec{a}{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{b} \times \vec{c}=\vec{c} \times\vec{a}{/tex} ...(ii)
From (i) and (ii), we get
{tex}\vec{a} \times\vec{b}=\vec{b} \times\vec{c}=\vec{c} \times\vec {a}{/tex}

question_html: Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} for which {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex} are perpendicular, where {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec b = 3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\lambda \hat{k}{/tex}.
question_text: Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} for which {tex}\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec b{/tex} are perpendicular, where {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec b = 3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\lambda \hat{k}{/tex} .
explanation: Given, {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec b = 3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\lambda \hat{k}{/tex} since these two vectors are perpendicular, therefore the dot product is zero,we {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}|{/tex} cos {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \frac{\pi}{2}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{\imath}+4 \hat{\jmath}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}) \cdot(3 \hat{\imath}-2 \hat{\jmath}+\lambda \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3)+(4 {tex}\times{/tex} -2)+(1 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} \lambda{/tex} ) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = - {tex} \lambda{/tex} + 6 - 8 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} -2 = {tex} \lambda{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex} \lambda{/tex} = -2
solution: Given, {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec b = 3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\lambda \hat{k}{/tex} since these two vectors are perpendicular, therefore the dot product is zero,we {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}|{/tex} cos {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \frac{\pi}{2}=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{\imath}+4 \hat{\jmath}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}) \cdot(3 \hat{\imath}-2 \hat{\jmath}+\lambda \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3)+(4 {tex}\times{/tex} -2)+(1 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} \lambda{/tex} ) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = - {tex} \lambda{/tex} + 6 - 8 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} -2 = {tex} \lambda{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex} \lambda{/tex} = -2
solution_html:
Given,{tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec b = 3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\lambda \hat{k}{/tex}
since these two vectors are perpendicular, therefore the dot product is zero,we 
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}|{/tex} cos {tex}\theta{/tex} = {tex}|\vec{a}||\vec{b}| \cos \frac{\pi}{2}=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}(2 \hat{\imath}+4 \hat{\jmath}-\hat{\mathrm{k}}) \cdot(3 \hat{\imath}-2 \hat{\jmath}+\lambda \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3)+(4 {tex}\times{/tex} -2)+(1 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} \lambda{/tex}) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = -{tex} \lambda{/tex} + 6 - 8 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} -2 = {tex} \lambda{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex} \lambda{/tex} = -2

question_html: Find a vector whose length is 3 and which is perpendicular to the vector {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-4 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=6 \hat{i}+5 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}
question_text: Find a vector whose length is 3 and which is perpendicular to the vector {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-4 \hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}=6 \hat{i}+5 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}
explanation: Given: {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-4 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec{b}=6 \hat{i}+5 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \vec a \times \vec{b}{/tex} {tex}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 3 & 1 & -4 \\ 6 & 5 & -2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}=(-2+20) \hat{i}-(-6+24) \hat{j}+(15-6) \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}=18 \hat{i}-18 \hat{j}+9 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=\sqrt{18^{2}+\left(-18^{2}\right)+9^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{729}{/tex} = 27 Required vector {tex}=3 \times\left\{\frac{\vec{a} \times \vec{b}}{|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|}\right\}{/tex} {tex}=3 \times \frac{18 \hat{i}-18 \hat{j}+9 \hat{k}}{27}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{3(2 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})}{3}{/tex} {tex}=2 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}
solution: Given: {tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-4 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\vec{b}=6 \hat{i}+5 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\therefore \vec a \times \vec{b}{/tex} {tex}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 3 & 1 & -4 \\ 6 & 5 & -2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}=(-2+20) \hat{i}-(-6+24) \hat{j}+(15-6) \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}=18 \hat{i}-18 \hat{j}+9 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=\sqrt{18^{2}+\left(-18^{2}\right)+9^{2}}{/tex} {tex}=\sqrt{729}{/tex} = 27 Required vector {tex}=3 \times\left\{\frac{\vec{a} \times \vec{b}}{|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|}\right\}{/tex} {tex}=3 \times \frac{18 \hat{i}-18 \hat{j}+9 \hat{k}}{27}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{3(2 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})}{3}{/tex} {tex}=2 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given:
{tex}\vec{a}=3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}-4 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\vec{b}=6 \hat{i}+5 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \vec a \times \vec{b}{/tex}{tex}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 3 & 1 & -4 \\ 6 & 5 & -2 \end{array}\right|{/tex}
{tex}=(-2+20) \hat{i}-(-6+24) \hat{j}+(15-6) \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}=18 \hat{i}-18 \hat{j}+9 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|=\sqrt{18^{2}+\left(-18^{2}\right)+9^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{729}{/tex}
= 27
Required vector {tex}=3 \times\left\{\frac{\vec{a} \times \vec{b}}{|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|}\right\}{/tex}
{tex}=3 \times \frac{18 \hat{i}-18 \hat{j}+9 \hat{k}}{27}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{3(2 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k})}{3}{/tex}
{tex}=2 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}

question_html: Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} for which the four points with position vectors {tex}(-\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} {tex}(2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-\hat{k}),(\hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+\hat{k}) \text { and }(3 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}) {/tex} are co planer.
question_text: Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} for which the four points with position vectors {tex}(-\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} {tex}(2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}-\hat{k}),(\hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+\hat{k}) \text { and }(3 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}) {/tex} are co planer.
explanation: Let position vector of A {tex}=-j+k{/tex} Let, position vector of C = {tex}\hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} Now, we ha {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}=\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{B}-\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{A}=2 \mathrm{i}-2 \mathrm{k}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{BC}=\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{B}=-\mathrm{i}+(\lambda+1) \mathrm{j}+2 \mathrm{k}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}}=\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{D}-\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{A}=4 i+2 \mathrm{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow[\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}}]=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left|\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & 0 & -2 \\ -1 & \lambda+1 & 2 \\ 4 & 0 & 2 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 0 \\ -1 & \lambda+1 & 3 \\ 4 & 0 & 6 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\left\{\mathrm{C}_{3} \rightarrow \mathrm{C}_{3}+\mathrm{C}_{1} \right\}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2\left|\begin{array}{cc} \lambda+1 & 3 \\ 0 & 6 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6(\lambda+1)-0=0 \Rightarrow 6 \lambda+6=0 \Rightarrow \lambda{/tex} = -1
solution: Let position vector of A {tex}=-j+k{/tex} Let, position vector of C = {tex}\hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} Now, we ha {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}=\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{B}-\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{A}=2 \mathrm{i}-2 \mathrm{k}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{BC}=\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{B}=-\mathrm{i}+(\lambda+1) \mathrm{j}+2 \mathrm{k}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}}=\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{D}-\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{A}=4 i+2 \mathrm{k}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow[\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}}]=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left|\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & 0 & -2 \\ -1 & \lambda+1 & 2 \\ 4 & 0 & 2 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 0 \\ -1 & \lambda+1 & 3 \\ 4 & 0 & 6 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\left\{\mathrm{C}_{3} \rightarrow \mathrm{C}_{3}+\mathrm{C}_{1} \right\}{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow 2\left|\begin{array}{cc} \lambda+1 & 3 \\ 0 & 6 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 6(\lambda+1)-0=0 \Rightarrow 6 \lambda+6=0 \Rightarrow \lambda{/tex} = -1
solution_html:
Let position vector of A  {tex}=-j+k{/tex}
Let, position vector of C = {tex}\hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}
Now, we ha{tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}=\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{B}-\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{A}=2 \mathrm{i}-2 \mathrm{k}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{BC}=\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{C}-\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{B}=-\mathrm{i}+(\lambda+1) \mathrm{j}+2 \mathrm{k}{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}}=\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{D}-\mathrm{P.V} \text { of } \mathrm{A}=4 i+2 \mathrm{k}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow[\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{BC}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}}]=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow\left|\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & 0 & -2 \\ -1 & \lambda+1 & 2 \\ 4 & 0 & 2 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex}  
{tex}\Rightarrow\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & 0 \\ -1 & \lambda+1 & 3 \\ 4 & 0 & 6 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} 
{tex}\left\{\mathrm{C}_{3} \rightarrow \mathrm{C}_{3}+\mathrm{C}_{1} \right\}{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 2\left|\begin{array}{cc} \lambda+1 & 3 \\ 0 & 6 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 6(\lambda+1)-0=0 \Rightarrow 6 \lambda+6=0 \Rightarrow \lambda{/tex} = -1

question_html: Find the volume of the parallele piped whose coterminous edges are represented by the vectors {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}-3 \hat{i}+\hat{k}, \vec{b}=\hat{i}-\hat{i}+2 \hat{k} \text { and } \vec{c}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{i}-\hat{k}{/tex}
question_text: Find the volume of the parallele piped whose coterminous edges are represented by the vectors {tex}\vec{a}=2 \hat{i}-3 \hat{i}+\hat{k}, \vec{b}=\hat{i}-\hat{i}+2 \hat{k} \text { and } \vec{c}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{i}-\hat{k}{/tex}
explanation: Here, I {tex}[\vec{a} \vec{b} \vec{c}]=\left|\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & -3 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & -1 \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{rrr} 0 & -1 & -3 \\ 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 0 & 3 & -5 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\left[R_{1} \rightarrow R_{1}-2 R_{2}, \text { and } R_{3} \rightarrow+^{1} R_{2}\right.{/tex} = (-1) (5. + 9) = -14 volume of the parallelepiped {tex}=|\vec{a}| \vec{b}|\vec{c}||=|-14 |=14{/tex} cubic units.
solution: Here, I {tex}[\vec{a} \vec{b} \vec{c}]=\left|\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & -3 & 1 \\ 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & -1 \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{rrr} 0 & -1 & -3 \\ 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 0 & 3 & -5 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\left[R_{1} \rightarrow R_{1}-2 R_{2}, \text { and } R_{3} \rightarrow+^{1} R_{2}\right.{/tex} = (-1) (5. + 9) = -14 volume of the parallelepiped {tex}=|\vec{a}| \vec{b}|\vec{c}||=|-14 |=14{/tex} cubic units.

question_html: Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} so that the four points with position vectors {tex}(-6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}),(3 \hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}),(5 \hat{i}+7 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}(-13 \hat{i}+17 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex}  are coplanar.
question_text: Find the value of {tex}\lambda{/tex} so that the four points with position vectors {tex}(-6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}),(3 \hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}),(5 \hat{i}+7 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}(-13 \hat{i}+17 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex} are coplanar.
explanation: Let the given points be A, B, C, D respectively. Then, {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}{/tex} = position vector of B - position vector of A {tex}(3 \hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+4 \hat{k})-(-6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} {tex}=9 \hat{i}+(\lambda-3) \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=(p . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } C)-(p . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } A){/tex} {tex}=(5 \hat{i}+7 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k})-(-6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k})=(11 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{A D}=(p . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } D)-(\mathbf{p} . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } A){/tex} {tex}=(-13 i+17 j-k)-(-6 i+3 j+2 k){/tex} {tex}=(-7 i+14 j-3 k){/tex} Now, A B C D are coplanar {tex}\Leftrightarrow[\overrightarrow{A B} ~~\overrightarrow{A C} ~~ \overrightarrow{A D}]=0 \Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rrrr} 9 & \lambda-3 & 2 \\ 11 & 4 & 1 \\ -7 & 14 & -3 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow 9(-12-14)-(\lambda-3)(-33+7)+2(154+28)=0{/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow-234+26 \lambda-78+364=0 \Leftrightarrow 26 \lambda=-52 \Leftrightarrow \lambda=-2{/tex} Hence, the required value of {tex}\lambda \text { is }-2{/tex}
solution: Let the given points be A, B, C, D respectively. Then, {tex}\overrightarrow{A B}{/tex} = position vector of B - position vector of A {tex}(3 \hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+4 \hat{k})-(-6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} {tex}=9 \hat{i}+(\lambda-3) \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=(p . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } C)-(p . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } A){/tex} {tex}=(5 \hat{i}+7 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k})-(-6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k})=(11 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{A D}=(p . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } D)-(\mathbf{p} . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } A){/tex} {tex}=(-13 i+17 j-k)-(-6 i+3 j+2 k){/tex} {tex}=(-7 i+14 j-3 k){/tex} Now, A B C D are coplanar {tex}\Leftrightarrow[\overrightarrow{A B} ~~\overrightarrow{A C} ~~ \overrightarrow{A D}]=0 \Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rrrr} 9 & \lambda-3 & 2 \\ 11 & 4 & 1 \\ -7 & 14 & -3 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow 9(-12-14)-(\lambda-3)(-33+7)+2(154+28)=0{/tex} {tex}\Leftrightarrow-234+26 \lambda-78+364=0 \Leftrightarrow 26 \lambda=-52 \Leftrightarrow \lambda=-2{/tex} Hence, the required value of {tex}\lambda \text { is }-2{/tex}
solution_html:
Let the given points be A, B, C, D respectively. Then,
{tex}\overrightarrow{A B}{/tex}  = position vector of B - position vector of A
{tex}(3 \hat{i}+\lambda \hat{j}+4 \hat{k})-(-6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}=9 \hat{i}+(\lambda-3) \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{A C}=(p . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } C)-(p . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } A){/tex}
{tex}=(5 \hat{i}+7 \hat{j}+3 \hat{k})-(-6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k})=(11 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{A D}=(p . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } D)-(\mathbf{p} . \mathbf{v} . \text { of } A){/tex}
{tex}=(-13 i+17 j-k)-(-6 i+3 j+2 k){/tex}
{tex}=(-7 i+14 j-3 k){/tex}
Now, A B C D are coplanar
{tex}\Leftrightarrow[\overrightarrow{A B} ~~\overrightarrow{A C} ~~ \overrightarrow{A D}]=0 \Leftrightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rrrr} 9 & \lambda-3 & 2 \\ 11 & 4 & 1 \\ -7 & 14 & -3 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow 9(-12-14)-(\lambda-3)(-33+7)+2(154+28)=0{/tex}
{tex}\Leftrightarrow-234+26 \lambda-78+364=0 \Leftrightarrow 26 \lambda=-52 \Leftrightarrow \lambda=-2{/tex}
Hence, the required value of {tex}\lambda \text { is }-2{/tex}

question_text: If {tex}\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}{/tex} are three non-coplanar vectors, then {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}) \cdot[(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \times(\vec{a}+\vec{c})]{/tex} equals
explanation: {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}) \cdot[(\vec{a}+\vec{b}) \times(\vec{a}+\vec{c})]{/tex} = {tex}(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}).((\vec{a}\times(\vec{a}+\vec{c})+\vec{b}\times(\vec{a}+\vec{c})){/tex} {tex}=(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}) \cdot(\vec{a} \times \vec{a}+\vec{a} \times \vec{c}+\vec{b} \times \vec{a}+\vec{b} \times \vec{c}){/tex} {tex}=(\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}) \cdot(\vec{a} \times \vec{c}+\vec{b} \times \vec{a}+\vec{b} \times \vec{c}){/tex} = {tex}\vec{a}.(\vec{a}\times\vec{c})+\vec{a}.(\vec{b}\times\vec{a})+\vec{a}.(\vec{b}\times\vec{c})+\vec{b}.(\vec{a}\times\vec{c})+\vec{b}.(\vec{b}\times\vec{c})+\vec{c}.(\vec{a}\times\vec{c})+\vec{c}.(\vec{b}\times\vec{a})+\vec{c}.(\vec{b}\times\vec{c}){/tex} {tex}=0+0+[\vec{a} \vec{b} \quad \vec{c}]+[\vec{b} \quad \vec{a} \quad \vec{c}]+0+0+0+[\vec{c} \quad \vec{b} \quad \vec{a}]+0{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{a} & \vec{b} & \vec{c} \end{array}\right]-[\vec{a} \vec{b} \quad \vec{c}]-[\vec{a} \vec{b} \vec{c}]{/tex} = {tex}-\left[\begin{array}{lll} \vec{a} & \vec{b} & \vec{c} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
